## PAGE 01122: Sync with Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `169674511`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`

top

##### Introduction

You can transfer data between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. After sync, the data on the Excel file of the selected sheet or CSV file matches the data in the modeling tool table. This mechanism is bidirectional and ensures continuous work between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables. If you don't have the ability to collaborate via the internet but you need to share information through Excel or CSV files, you can use this feature. The Excel/CSV file syncing is available in all tables of the modeling tool by using the **Excel/CSV** **Sync**button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Preparing Excel or CSV files to sync

To sync data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following prerequisites:

- The heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contain the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are the client and supplier of the relationship.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns. Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### How to synchronize your data

To synchronize the table with the Excel or CSV file

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an empty table' space='MD2024xR1'] or open an existing table.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1'] , select the Element Type or Classifier .
3. Click the **Columns** button and select properties and/or slots to which you want to sync data from the Excel or CSV file.
4. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1'], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Select File**. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024xR1']**Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog, select the Excel or CSV file either from your file system or from the model, if the file is already attached. [How to attach the file to a project >](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Attaching%2C+reattaching%2C+and+detaching+files) 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] An alternative way to link Excel or CSV file to the table is to drag it onto the table, hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag), then drop it, and select either**Link Excel/CSV File**or**Attach and Link Excel/CSV File**. If you want to modify sync options, select on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1'], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] ,then choose **Excel/CSV File** > **Sync Options**and go to step 6. If you want to synchronize data instantly in the ordinal position, go to step 9. - If you select another file, e.g. Vehicle Requirements.xlsx on a modeling tool table that is already linked with the file, e.g. Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx, the previous file named *Stakeholder Requirements.xlsx* is replaced with the latest one *Vehicle Requirements.xlsx*. 
- You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.
5. Click Details to expand the dialog.
6. The modeling tool might require refreshing the dialog before setting the mappings. Therefore, if you see this notification at the bottom of the dialog, click **Refresh**. Before uploading the mapping of the older version to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud Documentation' space='TWCloud2024xR1'], you need to recreate the mapping in the local project and resave it first.
7. In the opened **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog select options. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024xR1']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you are importing requirements, they are first mapped by ID if the ID column is displayed. If the ID column is not displayed, then the requirements are mapped by name. If the name is changed, then a new requirement is created in the table.
8. Click OK .
9. Import data from the linked file in one of the following ways: Additional optional procedures prior to data import[Using UML properties upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-UsingUMLpropertiesuponimportUsingUMLpropertiesuponimport)[Skipping empty rows and/or columns upon import](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Skippingemptyrowsand/orcolumnsuponimport) - The notification appears on the down-right corner of the modeling tool window (see the following figure), click **Read from File**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
-On the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Read From File** command. Read From File exceptionsAfter making any changes in the Excel or CSV file you must save them before using the**Read From File**command. Otherwise, the changes will not be imported to the modeling tool table.Data cannot be imported or imported correctly if:The CSV file format is invalid.Cells are non-editable.Data types of columns are not compatible with source information.Columns do not support information updates.Elements with the same names but different types exist.The owner is not set or updated because this element does not exist in the model.Also, some text formatting may not be transferred.
10. If you synchronize requirements, in the **Select Type** dialog, select the element you want to create in the modeling tool table.
11. Wait while data are imported into the table. The relevant notification displays in the down-right corner, when synchronization is completed. Items synchronized to the SysML requirements table appear in the Model Browser, too. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you made changes in the file and saved them, the table[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR1'] displays automatically and highlights the **New**, **Obsolete**, **Unchanged**, and **Updated** rows. In the figure above, the first requirement is updated, the seventh is newly created, and the second is deleted. All the items in the table are highlighted. The legend above reveals that all new items are highlighted like this. You can change the legend colors or hide the legend from the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
12. After making changes in the modeling tool table, export that data to the linked file as follows: on the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the **Write To File** command. Additional optional procedures prior to data export[Mapping elements by ID upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-ToexportbyIDMappingelementsbyIDuponexport)[Color marking modified data upon export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Managing+Excel+and+CSV+file#ManagingExcelandCSVfile-Colormarkingmodifieddatauponexport) Write To File exceptionsThe data cannot be exported or exported correctly if:The Excel file is corrupted.The CSV file is open.The CSV file is locked or you do not have the necessary permissions to access it. The data is synchronized between the modeling tool table and Excel or CSV file.

]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675951 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01123: Synchronization dialog environment

- page_id: `169675951`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675951/Synchronization+dialog+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-08-02T16:38:19.191+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Synchronization** dialog represents those elements whose parameters are not synchronized and provides the ability to restore the synchronization.

In this section, you will find brief information for opening the **Synchronization** dialog and for each button that can be useful while working in the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Synchronization dialog, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the invalid element. On the shortcut menu, point to Validation , then to the validation group, and click the Synchronize Manually command.
- On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='validation_invalid.png'] button, and then click the Synchronize Manually command.
- In the Validation Results window, click the Solve button and then click the Synchronize Manually command. For more information on how to open the Validation Results window, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1'] .

###### The source and target elements area

The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Source elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected source element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element. |
| Target elements area: |  |
| Up | Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Down | Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements. |
| Remove | Click to remove the selected target element from the project. |
| Edit | Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element. |

###### Buttons in the Synchronization dialog

The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Synchronize All | Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action) |
| Synchronize Selected | Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:Create -> - Click to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<-Create - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.Update -> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<-Update - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update. |

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Parameters synchronization' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog represents those elements whose parameters are not synchronized and provides the ability to restore the synchronization.</p><p>In this section, you will find brief information for opening the <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog and for each button that can be useful while working in the dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Synchronization dialog" ac:alt="Synchronization dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_synch.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Synchronization dialog environment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To open the Synchronization dialog, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the invalid element. On the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Validation</strong>, then to the validation group, and click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>On the diagram pane, select the invalid shape. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_invalid.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Synchronization dialog environment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button, and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, click the <strong>Solve</strong> button and then click the <strong>Synchronize Manually</strong> command. For more information on how to open the <strong>Validation Results</strong> window, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>The source and target elements area</h4><p>The following table describes the buttons in the source and target areas of the Synchronization dialog.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Source elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td>Up</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Down</td><td><p>Click to shift the selected source element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the source elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Remove</td><td><p>Click to remove the selected source element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Edit</td><td><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected source element.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><strong>Target elements area:</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Up</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element up a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Down</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to shift the selected target element down a row. In this way you can change the order of the target elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Remove</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to remove the selected target element from the project.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Edit</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Specification window of the selected target element.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Buttons in the Synchronization dialog</h4><p>The following table describes the Synchronization dialog buttons.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize All</strong></p></td><td><p>Click the button to synchronize all the not synchronized elements. Then from the menu select the straightforward or backward synchronization. For example, when synchronizing Parameters (Activity) and Pins (Call Behavior Action) you can select to synchronize one of the following:</p><ul><li>Pins (Call Behavior Action) by Parameters (Activity)</li><li>Parameters (Activity) by Pins (Call Behavior Action)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Synchronize Selected</strong></p></td><td><p>Click to synchronize the selected parameter. Note that the button is disabled if no parameters are selected in the list.</p><p>After you click the Synchronize Selected button, the following menu appears:<br /><strong>Create -&gt;</strong> - <strong>Click</strong> to create the target element with the same properties as the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Create</strong> - Click to create the source element to be created with the same properties as the target element.<br /><strong>Update -&gt;</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the target element according to the properties of the source element.<br /><strong>&lt;-Update</strong> - Click to update the not synchronized properties of the source element according to the properties of the target element.</p><p>Note that the Update command exists only if there are properties to update.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a36f92-37a4-4db1-a58d-8aea935ced28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="640cb8fa-31dd-4323-99bc-fa42a94cd8a6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44ba6805-c2bb-45ad-83b9-cbc485ec676b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678517 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01124: Syntax rules

- page_id: `169678517`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678517/Syntax+rules
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:27.840+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1333700293

1333700294

1333700293

- Command parameters containing spaces must be enclosed in quotes, for example, "UML Standard Profile".
- Command parameters are case sensitive, except project, imageFormat and recursive which are not case-sensitive.
- Blank spaces (“ “) separate commands and parameters.
- To specify a package name that contains a semicolon (;), you can use a backslash (\). For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.
- For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.
- A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.
- You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, "[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]" will be typed as "[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]".
- Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.
- If -upload is specified to the command line. Its value is validated and executed after a report has been generated.

1333700292

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Properties filename
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Using the Generate command to generate reports

For example, the package name “com;nomagic” can be typed by using “com\;nomagic”. The backslash followed by a semicolon (\;) is not considered to be a character separator.</li><li>For a parameter that contains unicode characters such as Thai, you can use the xml properties file to generate a report. The properties file does not support the unicode encoding.</li><li>A field entry consists of a name-value pair. The name and value formats are specified by [name=value] pattern strings. A valid name must start with a letter (a-z or A-Z) and can be followed by any letter, digit, or underscore.</li><li>You can specify a field name that consists of brackets ( [ ] ) by using backslashes ( \ ). For example, &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=[1.0]]&quot; will be typed as &quot;[author=NoMagic][Revision=\[1.0\]]&quot;.</li><li>Either -package or -element can be used with the Generate command. For example, you can use -package without -element.</li><li>If -upload is specified to the command line. Its value is validated and executed after a report has been generated.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10aeaa6d-401e-4206-bd71-a93468bbd0c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1333700292</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports">Using the Generate command to generate reports</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674223 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01125: Table Criteria area

- page_id: `169674223`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674223/Table+Criteria+area
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-06T14:37:58.919+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261086216

1261086218

1261086217

The **Criteria** area appears below the table toolbars. If it is not displayed, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the table toolbar to expand it.

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes in the **Criteria** area.

The following table describes the contents of the Criteria area according to different tables and provides references to procedures when trying to specify any box of this area.

| Table name where the box can be found | Box Name | Description | How to... |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Generic TableGlossary Table | Element Type | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog and select one or more types of elements you need to display in the Generic table. |  |
| Metric Table | Metric Suite | Click to open the Select Metric Suite dialog and select the metric suites you want to use for calculating the metrics of your model.If the Metric Table is created following the procedure described in , the Metric Suite box has one or more default values. Otherwise, you must specify the metric suite criteria to start working with the Metric Table. |  |
| Instance Table | Classifier | Click to open the Select Classifier dialog and select one or more classifiers whose instances you need to display in the Instance Table. |  |
| All tables | Scope (optional) | Click to select one or more packages, which contain the elements you need to display in the table.Click to open the Query dialog and specify the criteria that define your scope. |  |
| All tables | Filter | Type the phrase by which the element should be filtered and either press Enter or click the button.To open the menu with more filter options, click in the Filter box.To reset filter options, click in the Filter box. |  |

1261086215

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674200 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01126: Table toolbars

- page_id: `169674200`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674200/Table+toolbars
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T17:33:52.790+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1260269502

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1260269505

1260269500

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1260269504

#### NOTE: Important

Important

If the table toolbars are not available in a server project, lock the table for editing. Make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project.

##### Basic toolbar buttons

The following table describes the toolbar buttons found in tables.

| Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Navigation toolbar |  |  |
|  | Alt+B | Opens the in the and selects the current diagram or symbol in the Containment tree. |
| Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Add New > Create | Insert | Click to create a new element in the table. This element will also be added to the model. If several element types are available, a shortcut menu with available types will open. You will be able to select an element of the type you need.NoteIn Instance Table, expand and select to add a new instance. If this button is not active, the classifier is not specified for the instance table.In Metric Table, click Calculate Metrics and select Add New Metric with Different Parameters to create a new row with copied parameters. |
| Add Existing | Ctrl+Insert | Click to add an element from a model. The Select Element dialog opens. Only elements of the type defined in a specific table will be listed.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Delete | Ctrl+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the table and the model.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Remove From Table | Ctrl+Delete | Click to remove only selected elements from the table.NoteIn Instance Table, expand to find this button. |
| Layout toolbar |  |  |
|  |  | Click to expand or collapse rows when the Display Mode under the Options button is selected as Compact tree or Complete tree.You may expand the command and select:Expand Selected Recursively - to expand the selected row recursively.Expand All - to expand all rows.Collapse Selected Recursively - to collapse the selected row recursively.Collapse All - to collapse all rows. |
|  | Ctrl+Up Arrow | Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) up a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving. |
|  | Ctrl+Down Arrow | Click to shift selected elements (either grouped or non-grouped) down a row. The elements are automatically renumbered after moving. |
| Columns | N/A | Click to change the set of columns to show on the table. |
| Publish toolbar |  |  |
| Export | N/A | Click to export the contents of the table to an *.html, *.csv, or *.xlsx file.NoteIn the Metric Table this button also allows you export to *.xlsm file. |
| Excel/CSV Sync | N/A | Click to open the Excel/CSV Sync menu:Excel/CSV File - contains three commands: - Select File - opens the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog and allows you to select a file from your file system or from your model to link it with the modeling tool table and enable data syncing. - Clear Selection - removes the reference between the modeling tool table and linked file and syncing is turned off. - Open File - opens the linked file.Read from File - imports data from Excel/CSV file to modeling tool table.Write to File - exports data from modeling tool table to Excel/CSV file.Sync Options - opens the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog and allows you to customize sync and mapping options before syncing data between Excel/CSV file and modeling tool table. |
| Find in Diagram |  |  |
| Find in Diagram | Ctrl+F | Click to open the search bar allowing you to search for textual information in all types of diagrams, including tables, matrices, and maps. Use / to navigate the search results. |
| View toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to update the contents of the table after specifying the scope.NoteIn the Instance Table, this button updates data after specifying the smart package as the scope criteria. |
| Validation toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to validate the table against all validation rules stored in the model, except those that are ignored. |
| Options toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to open the Options menu. Each table contains different options under this button. Below are the meanings of all available options:Show Full Paths – displays the full paths of elements (where owner information can be displayed).Save Filter Criteria – keeps the unchanged after reloading the project or after restarting the modeling tool.Show Detailed Column Name – in a generic table, shows property group and/or stereotype names in the table header. This is helpful when the table displays several columns with the same name. In a metric table, it shows the names of metric suites in column headers but it works when the metric table represents the metrics of two or more metric suites. In an instance table, it shows classifier names in column headers.Show Column Type – in a metric table, shows types of metric and parameter values in column headers. For example, Blocks Count : Integer. In an instance table, it shows types of slot values in column headers. For example, Year : Integer or Boat.Beam : String.Show Column Icons - shows icons in the column headers.Show Abbreviation in Column Type – in a metric table, shows abbreviations of metric or parameter names in column headers, if they are specified.Show Units on Values - in an instance table, shows unit symbols next to values.Show Units on Columns - in an instance table, it shows unit symbols in column headers.Wrap Text - wraps long text in table cells. The option is disabled by default. Limit Row Height - strips cell entries that cover more than 10 element values or plain text lines. Three dots are added at the end of the stripped cell. The complete text is available in the tooltip or in the cell edit mode.Load Partially - limits data calculation and loading to only visible cells in tables.Include Additional Content - includes additional content of elements that fall within the specified scope in tables. For example, if a Smart Package comes within the scope of the table, its content is collected and represented in the table.Enable Patterns-Based Verification - performs patterns-based Requirements verification.Display Mode - allows selection of one of the following data visualization options:- Complete tree - displays elements hierarchically with all their owners in the column header.- Compact tree - displays elements hierarchically with their direct and common owners in the column header.To learn more, visit the page.- List - displays elements in a plain list.Use Acronyms - recognizes acronyms everywhere in the text. Only capitalized terms are recognized as acronyms. Lowercase words are not interpreted as acronyms.Specification – opens the of an appropriate table. |
| Suppress/Expand Criteria Area toolbar |  |  |
|  | N/A | Click to hide the Criteria area. |
|  | N/A | Click to show the Criteria area. |
| Other buttons |  |  |
|  | Alt+Left Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map. |
|  | Alt+Right Arrow | Click to navigate to the previously opened diagram, table, dependency matrix, or relation map. |

##### Specific toolbar buttons

The following table describes the toolbar buttons found only in specific tables.

| Table name where button can be found | Button | Shortcut keys | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Edit toolbar |  |  |
| Instance Table | > Create with Parts | Alt+Insert | Click to create a new instance with parts. |
| Instance Table | > Delete with Parts | Alt+D | Click to remove selected elements both from the table and the model. |
| Metric Table | Calculate Metrics | N/A | Click to open the Calculate Metrics menu and then select the . |
| Requirement Table | Add Nested | Alt+Insert | Click to add a nested Requirement in the model. |
|  | Layout toolbar |  |  |
| Metric Table | Show Metric Suites | N/A | Click to select the metric suites whose columns you need to show on the Metric table. |
|  | Other buttons |  |  |
| Glossary Table |  | N/A | Click to turn the glossary on/off in the project. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674726 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01127: Table with diagram information

- page_id: `169674726`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674726/Table+with+diagram+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-23T15:49:49.328+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

569464989

569464992

569464991

On the diagram, you can display a table containing various diagram details: its name, author, status, the dates it was created and modified, etc. By default, the Diagram info table is displayed at the right top corner of the diagram frame, but you can drag and drop it to any other position on the diagram.

The table includes the following fields:

- Diagram name
- Author
- Creation date
- Modification date
- Last Modified by
- Other available tag definitions

To show the table containing the diagram information

- From the diagram shortcut menu, select Show Diagram Info .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To define information for the table

1. Do one of the following:
  - From the diagram information table shortcut menu, select Customize.
  - From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog > Diagram Info > Source , select the type of information you want to include in the table:
  - Standard Mode . This mode contains the following fields that will be shown in the table: Author, Creation date, Modification date, and all other tag definitions that can be assigned to the diagram.
  - Custom Mode . In this mode, you can create your own table or any other object in HTML.
3. Preview the selected table or other created object in the Preview .

569464988

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

etc. By default, the Diagram info table is displayed at the right top corner of the diagram frame, but you can drag and drop it to any other position on the diagram.</p><p>The table includes the following fields:</p><ul><li>Diagram name</li><li>Author</li><li>Creation date</li><li>Modification date</li><li>Last Modified by</li><li>Other available tag definitions</li></ul><p> </p><p>To show the table containing the diagram information</p><hr /><ul><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Diagram Info</strong>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="digraminfo.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table with diagram information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To define information for the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>From the diagram information table shortcut menu, select <strong>Customize.</strong></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the Project Options dialog &gt; <strong>Diagram Info</strong> &gt; <strong>Source</strong>, select the type of information you want to include in the table: <ul><li><strong>Standard Mode</strong>. This mode contains the following fields that will be shown in the table: Author, Creation date, Modification date, and all other tag definitions that can be assigned to the diagram. </li><li><strong>Custom Mode</strong>. In this mode, you can create your own table or any other object in HTML.</li></ul></li><li>Preview the selected table or other created object in the <strong>Preview</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ff23793-ea88-4a9f-a379-0783a6827205"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">569464988</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678237 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01128: Tableprop tool

- page_id: `169678237`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678237/Tableprop+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-02-22T05:36:15.858+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1586165640

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1586165642

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1586165641

**$tableprop** is a table property tool to manage table style. In this version, you can set merged rows vertically and columns horizontally in *DOCX*,*PPTX*, and*XLSX* templates using **$tableprop**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This statement must be defined in a table.

1586165638

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f94fe3a4-4f5d-4bf5-8c07-ca904fca6d8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3826422-1139-4ef2-b3db-c748e444aca2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165642</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecd21a5a-8560-4a3d-a68a-00cf03e27480"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165641</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>$tableprop</strong> is a table property tool to manage table style. In this version, you can set merged rows vertically and columns horizontally in <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">DOCX</em>,<em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> PPTX</em>, and<em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> XLSX</em> templates using <strong>$tableprop</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a02093-3a51-4cf2-9982-031e8a405d48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This statement must be defined in a table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2761a5c5-e3dc-4a2f-8a6f-a71177479064"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1586165638</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="599b290e-aba8-4f32-9142-1807ef9dcbed" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674185 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01129: Tables

- page_id: `169674185`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674185/Tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:28:22.414+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1259844527

1259844530

1259844526

**On this page**

43

1259844528

##### Introduction

You can use modeling tool tables to represent and modify information about the properties of model elements and/or relationships between them. You can create four kinds of tables: Generic Table, Glossary Table, Metric Table, and Instance Table. The different purposes for each table are illustrated below:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024xR1'] allows you to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place. The example below is from a [ATTACHMENT filename='generic table.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='MD2024xR1']Glossary Table allows you to define the specific term(s) and gives you the ability to reuse the term(s) anywhere in your model. The example below is created only for this image and is not saved in any of the sample models. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024xR1'] allows you to manage an Instance Specification of your model in spreadsheet form. The example is from [ATTACHMENT filename='SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space='MD2024xR1']Metric Table allows you to collect various metrics used to evaluate the current state of your model. The example below is from [ATTACHMENT filename='MagicLibrary requirements.mdzip'] sample model. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Table areas

Before working with tables, you should understand their structure. All tables contain the following areas:

- *Table toolbars* allow you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- *Criteria area* allows you to define the elements that you want to display in the table, specify the scope of the table, and filter necessary information in tables that contain large amounts of data. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- Grid displays data according to the defined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1'] . You can modify the displayed data in the grid by editing cells, removing, hiding, creating new, or selecting other options from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1'] . The grid consists of: - Rows that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table row represents an element . 
- Columns that appear along the top of the table, and are labeled using element property names. Each table column represents element properties. - Cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element property values.
- Description area allows you to view the general information regarding the purpose and functions of the table.
- Table info area allows you to view the table display information. On the left, you can see the number of displayed and total rows; on the right, the status, indicating whether the table is still loading [ATTACHMENT filename='status_loading_2.png'] or fully loaded [ATTACHMENT filename='status_loading_complete_2.png'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1259860030

**Basic features of tables**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Basic tasks in tables' space='MD2024xR1']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The example is from <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> sample model.</p><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Metric table" /><ac:link-body> <span class="confluence-link">Metric Table</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> allows you to collect various metrics used to evaluate the current state of your model. The example below is from <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary requirements.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> sample model.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul><h3>Table areas</h3><p>Before working with tables, you should understand their structure. All tables contain the following areas:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"> <em>Table toolbars</em> </span> allow you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about table toolbars >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <em>Criteria area</em> </span> allows you to define the elements that you want to display in the table, specify the scope of the table, and filter necessary information in tables that contain large amounts of data. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Criteria area >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><em>Grid</em> displays data according to the defined <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can modify the displayed data in the grid by editing cells, removing, hiding, creating new, or selecting other options from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Table toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The grid consists of:<br /><em>- Rows</em> that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table column <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">represents element properties. </span> <em> <br />- Cells</em> are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element property values.</li><li><em>Description area</em> allows you to view the general information regarding the purpose and functions of the table. </li><li><em>Table info area</em> allows you to view the table display information. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675469 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01130: Tag

- page_id: `169675469`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675469/Tag
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-07-09T22:09:10.502+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Just like a class, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']can have properties, which can be referred to as tag definitions. When a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']is applied to a model element, the values of the properties may be referred to as tagged values.

The tag definitions are used to define new meta attributes of the extended metaclass, they are used as regular class attributes.

An actual instance of the tag definition is a tagged value (tag).

A tag holds extra information like:

- additional information that does not come with UML, for example Precondition for Use Cases.
- management data about the state and progress of the project such as author, status, and tested.
- language specific data for code generation tools.

A tagged value consists of two parts: name and value (example: Author = Joe).

To create a new tag definition

1. Create a new stereotype.
2. Open the stereotype [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] .
3. In the Tag Definitions property group, click the Create button to add a new tag definition for stereotype.
4. Select the type of this property. Type of the property can be a standard UML data type or another user defined Stereotype. Regular classes should not be used as types of tag definition.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673111 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01131: Tag type

- page_id: `169673111`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673111/Tag+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-11T09:14:42.632+02:00`
- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

TagType

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the Tag type is not a user-level type.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Property

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the Tag type is not a user-level type.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Stereotype *Car*has a Tag Definition *gearTransmission*typed by the Class *Gear Transmissions*, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by changing the Tag Definition's type to an Enumeration element *Gear Transmissions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>TagType</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the Tag type is not a user-level type.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Property</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the Tag type is not a user-level type.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Untitled.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tag type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Stereotype <em>Car </em>has a Tag Definition <em>gearTransmission </em>typed by the Class <em>Gear Transmissions</em>, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by changing the Tag Definition's type to an Enumeration element <em>Gear Transmissions</em>.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673109 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01132: Tags Multiplicity

- page_id: `169673109`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673109/Tags+Multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-11T08:58:42.107+02:00`
- ancestors: UML correctness rules (in progress)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

TagMult1

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the number of specified Tagged Values does not exceed the multiplicity defined for the Tag Definition.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Tagged Value

**Solvers**

- Remove last value(s) - removes the Tag Value(s) starting from the last one, leaving only the number of values corresponding to the specified multiplicity.
- Increase multiplicity - increases the Tag Definition's multiplicity to match the number of Tagged Values.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**Stereotype *Car* has a Tag Definition *gearTransmission*with the multiplicity set to one. The Class *Model 1*with the applied stereotype *Car*has the Tag *gearTransmission*specified with two values, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved via the **Remove last value(s)**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>TagMult1</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the number of specified Tagged Values does not exceed the multiplicity defined for the Tag Definition.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Tagged Value</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Remove last value(s) </strong>- removes the Tag Value(s) starting from the last one, leaving only the number of values corresponding to the specified multiplicity.  </li><li><strong>Increase multiplicity </strong>- increases the Tag Definition's multiplicity to match the number of Tagged Values.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tags Multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tags Multiplicity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>Stereotype <em>Car</em> has a Tag Definition <em>gearTransmission </em>with the multiplicity set to one. The Class <em>Model 1 </em>with the applied stereotype <em>Car </em>has the Tag <em>gearTransmission </em>specified with two values, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved via the <strong>Remove last value(s) </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676986 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01133: Tags multiplicity 2

- page_id: `169676986`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676986/Tags+multiplicity+2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-18T09:14:03.951+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

TagMult2

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the number of tag values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

TaggedValue

**Solvers**

- To fix this, make sure the number of tag values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.

**Example**

###### **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**The Stereotype *Car* has a tag definition *airbags* with a multiplicity of two or more. The class *Car Model 1*with the applied stereotype *Car*has the *airbags*tag specified with only one value, which causes an error. 
The error is resolved by specifying two values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>TagMult2</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the number of tag values is in the multiplicity range. The validation rule is triggered if there are fewer values than the set multiplicity range.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>TaggedValue</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li>To fix this, make sure the number of tag values is not lower than the specified multiplicity range.</li></ul><p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="844f9cdb-b9ab-4de6-af39-8d12da834add">Example</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tags multiplicity 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tags multiplicity 2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong>The Stereotype <em>Car</em> has a tag definition <em>airbags</em> with a multiplicity of two or more. The class <em>Car Model 1 </em>with the applied stereotype <em>Car </em>has the <em>airbags </em>tag specified with only one value, which causes an error.<br />The error is resolved by specifying two values.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673065 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01134: Tags Spelling

- page_id: `169673065`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673065/Tags+Spelling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-18T18:02:58.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Spelling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

TagSpellError

**Description**

This validation rule checks if a Tagged Value has a spelling error.

**Severity**

error

**Constrained Element**

Element

**Solvers**

- Correct - opens the Spell Checker dialog allowing you to correct the spelling error.

**Example**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The element *Model 1* has a Tag *gearTransmission*with the Tagged Value *Manuaal*, containing a spelling error that triggers the validation error. 
The error is resolved by correcting the Tagged Value text.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>TagSpellError</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if a Tagged Value has a spelling error.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>error</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Element</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Correct </strong>- opens the Spell Checker dialog allowing you to correct the spelling error.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tags_Spelling.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Tags Spelling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The element <em>Model 1</em> has a Tag <em>gearTransmission </em>with the Tagged Value <em>Manuaal</em>, containing a spelling error that triggers the validation error. <br />The error is resolved by correcting the Tagged Value text. </h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677587 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01135: Teamwork Cloud data markings

- page_id: `169677587`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677587/Teamwork+Cloud+data+markings
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:26.712+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Teamwork Cloud data markings help to classify resources with custom-defined access levels. They are shown if they were [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='TWCloud2024xR1'].

You may see Teamwork Cloud data markings when you:

- try to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024xR1'] ,
- want to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing server projects' space='MD2024xR1'] , or
- need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024xR1'] in your project.

If set, Teamwork Cloud data markings are displayed in an appropriate column of a dialog:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Open Server Project dialog with Teamwork Cloud data markings.

If there is an access level set on a project and you want to use that project in your project, your project must have the same or more strict access level rights. Otherwise, you see a warning:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you decide to use a used project with a stricter access level in your project, users must also have the same or stricter access level rights to be able to open your project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Teamwork Cloud data markings help to classify resources with custom-defined access levels. They are shown if they were <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabled and created in Teamwork Cloud Admin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You may see Teamwork Cloud data markings when you:</p><ul><li>try to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Opening projects from Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open a server project from Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>want to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing server projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[manage a server project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or</li><li>need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ use a project ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in your project.</li></ul><p>If set, Teamwork Cloud data markings are displayed in an appropriate column of a dialog:</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_data_markings.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud data markings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Open Server Project dialog with Teamwork Cloud data markings.</h6><p>If there is an access level set on a project and you want to use that project in your project, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="55676b24-9d82-4260-b1db-87d21b28b79a">your project</ac:inline-comment-marker> must have the same or more strict access level rights. Otherwise, you see a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ad51715f-9f21-4817-b4be-692f914795d7">warning</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="156" ac:width="639"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_warning.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Teamwork Cloud data markings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you decide to use a used project with a stricter access level in your project, users must also have the same or stricter access level rights to be able to open your project.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675471 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01136: Template

- page_id: `169675471`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675471/Template
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:10.684+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Working With Templates

502366679

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Working With Templates

502366682

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Working With Templates

502366680

Use a Template to define concrete types for elements without concrete types. You can create a template and then use it as many times as you need. Moreover, when you have elements of the same structure (or with the same parameters) but with different values, the template you use can easily define parameters for each element. You can apply template parametrization to classifiers, packages, and operations. Most often. template parametrization is used for classes and interfaces. Templates are often used in code engineering (Java and other programming languages).

The template consists of:

- A parameterized element (an element with defined template parameters). The parameterized element can also be called a template element.
- An actual element (an element for which a template is applied, an element with actual values). The actual element can also be called a templateable element.
- A template binding relationship (a relationship from the actual element to the parameterized element).
- A template parameter substitution (used to define actual parameters of the actual element that substitutes formal template parameters). Template parameter substitutions are created on the template binding relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Template

In this example, you can see the *GeneralArray*template class, which has the *ArraySize* and *ArrayType* template parameters, represented by the dashed rectangle in the upper-right corner of the class. These parameters represent the size and type of the general array.The attribute *GeneralArray::contents : ArrayType [0..ArraySize]* represents the *ArrayType* and the *ArraySize* template parameters. The *ArraySize* template parameter is of the Integer type, and its default value is 10. The *ArrayType* template parameter is of the Class type (it is not represented in the shape), and its default value is not specified. The default value of the template parameter is used if no actual value is supplied for the parameter in a binding. Typically, the parameter types are classifiers, but they can also be integers or other types.

The *GeneralArray* class and its template parameters create a template of the general array of undefined types. You can use this template in other concrete arrays as many times as you need.

How to use the template and how to create an actual element

To create an array of addresses, you would create the *AddressArray* class. Alone (without a template), the *AddressArray* class would have undefined types. A template binding is created from the *AddressArray* class to the *GeneralArray* class. Now the template binding connection points to the *GeneralArray* template class.

The template binding specifies template parameter substitutions, the actual values of parameters for the address array. In the example, we created the following actual values of the AdressArray class: the array size having the value 3 and the array of the *Address* type.

The output of these definitions results in the *AddressArray* class having the attribute *AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3]*.

Attributes for the actual element classes should be created manually. In the example, the*AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3]*attribute is not created automatically - you must create this attribute manually. Use these optional solutions to create these attributes in your model or not.

#### TIP: Working With Templates

Working With Templates

Step by step instructions for modelling a template in the modeling tool are described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Template' space='MD2024xR1'].

You can format template parameter symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024xR1']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can specify template parameter properties in the Template Parameter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] and template binding properties in the Template Binding Specification window. In the specification window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

502366678

**Related pages**

- Model elements
- Auxiliary Diagram Symbols
- Diagramming

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can create a template and then use it as many times as you need. Moreover, when you have elements of the same structure (or with the same parameters) but with different values, the template you use can easily define parameters for each element. You can apply template parametrization to classifiers, packages, and operations. Most often. template parametrization is used for classes and interfaces. Templates are often used in code engineering (Java and other programming languages).</p><p>The template consists of:</p><ul><li>A parameterized element (an element with defined template parameters). The parameterized element can also be called a template element.</li><li>An actual element (an element for which a template is applied, an element with actual values). The actual element can also be called a templateable element.</li><li>A template binding relationship (a relationship from the actual element to the parameterized element).</li><li>A template parameter substitution (used to define actual parameters of the actual element that substitutes formal template parameters). Template parameter substitutions are created on the template binding relationship.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="template_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of Template</h6><p>In this example, you can see the <em>GeneralArray </em>template class, which has the <em>ArraySize</em> and <em>ArrayType</em> template parameters, represented by the dashed rectangle in the upper-right corner of the class. The default value of the template parameter is used if no actual value is supplied for the parameter in a binding. Typically, the parameter types are classifiers, but they can also be integers or other types.</p><p>The <em>GeneralArray</em> class and its template parameters create a template of the general array of undefined types. You can use this template in other concrete arrays as many times as you need.</p><p><u>How to use the template and how to create an actual element</u></p><p>To create an array of addresses, you would create the <em>AddressArray</em> class. Alone (without a template), the <em>AddressArray</em> class would have undefined types. A template binding is created from the <em>AddressArray</em> class to the <em>GeneralArray</em> class. Now the template binding connection points to the <em>GeneralArray</em> template class.</p><p>The template binding specifies template parameter substitutions, the actual values of parameters for the address array. In the example, we created the following actual values of the AdressArray class: the array size having the value 3 and the array of the <em>Address</em> type.</p><p>The output of these definitions results in the <em>AddressArray</em> class having the attribute <em>AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3]</em>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d09d8263-e3ce-42d5-8809-3c30674fc9b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Attributes for the actual element classes should be created manually. In the example, the </span><em>AddressArray::contents : Address [0..3] </em><span>attribute is not created automatically - you must create this attribute manually. Use these optional solutions to create these attributes in your model or not. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abc8cd1a-cec0-4421-acda-39dc44cb9904"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Working With Templates</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Step by step instructions for modelling a template in the modeling tool are described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Working with Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working With Templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can format template parameter symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><span>You can specify template parameter properties in the Template Parameter <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> and template binding properties in the Template Binding Specification window. In the specification window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f276abb8-02ea-4ad7-bcee-aa487fc86035"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">502366678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Model+elements">Model elements</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Auxiliary+diagram+symbols">Auxiliary Diagram Symbols</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Diagramming">Diagramming</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
