<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678457 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01207: Uploading reports to remote locations

- page_id: `169678457`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678457/Uploading+reports+to+remote+locations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-04-01T12:25:50.543+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1336874444

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1336874446

1336950205

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1336874445

You can use the Report Wizard to upload a generated report to a predefined remote location server. Each remote location server has a profile that holds all necessary information, e.g., protocol, URL, username, and password. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile Management dialog' space='MD2024xR1'] is used to create, edit, or delete profiles containing the configuration for remote location servers.

In this section, you will learn how to create a new remote location server step by step to upload a generated report to a newly created server, and how to upload a generated report to an existing server.

##### Uploading reports to a new server

To upload a generated report to a newly created server

1. Open the Report Wizard dialog. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the Output options step. In the Report Wizard dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Three-dot image.png'] in the Publish to server area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. The **Profile Management** dialog opens. Click **New** to create a new server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. The **New server profile** dialog opens. Enter the name of the server to create. You cannot leave the **Name** box empty or assign a name that has already been used in an existing set of servers. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
4. Choose a protocol from the **Protocol** drop-down list and fill out a URL address in the **Address** box. You can leave out the **User Name** and/or **Password** boxes if desired. Click **Save Profiles** to save the newly added server, and close the **Profile Management** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you leave out the **User Name** and/or **Password** boxes in the **Profile Management** dialog when creating a new server, you must enter the authentication information in the **Authentication** dialog after the report generation, then click **OK** to proceed.

5. You are now back to the **Report Wizard** dialog. Select the newly created server from the **Select server** drop-down list. Click **Generate** to generate the report to upload it to the newly created server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously created.

6. In case the server has not been trusted, the dialog will be opened asking to accept or reject the server certificate. Accept the server certificate for publishing the generated report to this server, or reject the server certificate to end the publishing report process.

7. If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Uploading reports to an existing server

To upload a generated report to an existing server

1. Open the Report Wizard dialog. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the Output options step. Select an existing server from the Select server drop-down list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Click **Generate** to generate the report to upload it to the chosen existing server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously selected.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the selected existing server does not have the username and/or password specified in the **Profile Management** dialog when the server was created, you must enter the authentication information in the **Authentication** dialog after the report generation, then click **OK** to proceed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This feature does not support SharePoint Server.

1336874443

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile Management dialog' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb21db6a-13fc-4e02-950f-fb9f41f556c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336874444</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55d1206b-dad9-4733-afdc-70200fb9381e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336874446</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24a9ea7e-e66b-4b4f-bd9a-7f9801182c19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336950205</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1396c1c1-35a2-42fa-b7fe-972fedc11e5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbd78dd0-2fcb-4d97-b47d-107d844455a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336874445</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use the Report Wizard to upload a generated report to a predefined remote location server. Each remote location server has a profile that holds all necessary information, e.g., protocol, URL, username, and password. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Profile Management dialog" /></ac:link> is used to create, edit, or delete profiles containing the configuration for remote location servers.</p><p>In this section, you will learn how to create a new remote location server step by step to upload a generated report to a newly created server, and how to upload a generated report to an existing server.</p><h3>Uploading reports to a new server</h3><p>To upload a generated report to a newly created server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog. On the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the <strong>Output options</strong> step. In the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Three-dot image.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the <strong>Publish to server</strong> area.<br /><br /></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Dialog for Uploading Report to Remote Server" ac:alt="Dialog for Uploading Report to Remote Server"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rep-wiz-output-options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. The <strong>Profile Management</strong> dialog opens. Click <strong>New</strong> to create a new server.<br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="web-server-connect.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. The <strong>New server profile</strong> dialog opens. Enter the name of the server to create. You cannot leave the <strong>Name</strong> box empty or assign a name that has already been used in an existing set of servers. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="New Server Profile Dialog" ac:alt="New Server Profile Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="web-server-connect-b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br />4. Choose a protocol from the <strong>Protocol</strong> drop-down list and fill out a URL address in the <strong>Address</strong> box. You can leave out the <strong>User Name</strong> and/or <strong>Password</strong> boxes if desired. Click <strong>Save Profiles</strong> to save the newly added server, and close the <strong>Profile Management</strong> dialog.<br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Enter Server Details" ac:alt="Enter Server Details"><ri:attachment ri:filename="web-server-connect-c.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e553a68-477c-4cba-b56a-13786333fdeb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you leave out the <strong>User Name</strong> and/or <strong>Password</strong> boxes in the <strong>Profile Management</strong> dialog when creating a new server, you must enter the authentication information in the <strong>Authentication</strong> dialog after the report generation, then click <strong>OK</strong> to proceed.</p><p><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1588 No Username and Password.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br />5. You are now back to the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog. Select the newly created server from the <strong>Select server</strong> drop-down list. Click <strong>Generate</strong> to generate the report to upload it to the newly created server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously created.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">6. In case the server has not been trusted, the dialog will be opened asking to accept or reject the server certificate. Accept the server certificate for publishing the generated report to this server, or reject the server certificate to end the publishing report process.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">7. If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1588 Successful Upload.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3>Uploading reports to an existing server</h3><p>To upload a generated report to an existing server</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog. On the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. Proceed through each step by selecting a report template, report data, and Element scope. You will be at the <strong>Output options</strong> step. Select an existing server from the <strong>Select server</strong> drop-down list.<br /><br /></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1588 Report Generated and Upload to Profile.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Click <strong>Generate</strong> to generate the report to upload it to the chosen existing server. Once the report has been generated, it will be uploaded to the server previously selected.<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3855990d-0298-465e-912a-7a7c14a9a419"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the selected existing server does not have the username and/or password specified in the <strong>Profile Management</strong> dialog when the server was created, you must enter the authentication information in the <strong>Authentication</strong> dialog after the report generation, then click <strong>OK</strong> to proceed.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1588 No Username and Password.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br />3.  If the report has been successfully uploaded, a message box informing that the resources have been uploaded successfully opens. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1588 Successful Upload.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Uploading reports to remote locations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80fe233c-8406-4a19-8075-61fe55baa0ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature does not support SharePoint Server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f973f26d-f069-4ce0-8d47-58f87cf34ded"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336874443</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Profile Management dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676285 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01208: Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog

- page_id: `169676285`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676285/Usages+and+Dependencies+Search+Options+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-09-16T06:48:37.056+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Analyzing usages and dependencies
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Usage information

1389154636

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Usage information

1389154638

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Usage information

1389154637

INLINE

Use the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog to find model elements that either use the selected element, or are used by it. You can search using different sets of criteria and analyze the search results to understand the relationships between model elements.

To open the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog

- In the model browser or a diagram, right-click the element whose usages or dependencies you want to find out, and select Related Elements > Used by . The dialog allows specifying the search options to find the elements that use the selected elements in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- In the model browser or a diagram, right-click the element whose usages or dependencies you want to find out, and select Related Elements > Depends On . The dialog allows specifying the search options to find model elements that are used by the selected elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog with the default search criteria selected.

The following table describes the search options of the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options**dialog.

| Dialog element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Load autoloadable used projects | Select this check box to include unloaded used projects in the usages search scope. Before starting the search, you can select the unloaded projects you want to load.This check box is available only if you select the Used By command. |
| Search recursively | Select this check box to search for usages/dependencies for the selected element and all the elements it owns. |
| Ignore derived properties | Select this check box to exclude the of the selected element from the usages/dependencies search results.It is recommended to select this check box, as including derived properties in the search scope may cause performance downgrade. |
| Ignore inter interrelations | Select this check box to exclude the usages/dependencies between the elements owned by the selected element from the usages/dependencies search results. This option allows you to analyze how the selected element and its owned elements are related to the outer elements (not owned by the selected element) of the model.Usage informationThis option is only effective if you select the Search recursively check box as well. |
| Search in diagrams with symbols only | Select this check box to check the element usage in diagrams containing symbols only. |
| Level of details | Select one of the predefined values (Classifiers or Packages), or click and select desired element types to define the level of details. If you select element types manually, the usages/dependencies search results will only display the elements of these types. |
| Treat relationships as search result | Select this check box to find a complete list of used/dependent relationships together with other results. |
| Treat model elements at the end of relationships as search result | Select this check box to exclude relationships from search results and only find the usages/dependencies connected with these relationships. |
| Show results in the new tab | Select this check box to display the results of each search in a new tab. |
| Show this dialog before searching | Select this check box to open the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog before a new search is started. If you leave the check box empty, the dialog will not open, and the next search will use the search options specified during your last search.To open the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog, click on the toolbar of the usages/dependencies search results window. |

1389154634

INLINE

If you select element types manually, the usages/dependencies search results will only display the elements of these types.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Treat relationships as search result</strong></p></td><td><p>Select this check box to find a complete list of used/dependent relationships together with other results.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Treat model elements at the end of relationships as search result</strong></p></td><td><p>Select this check box to exclude relationships from search results and only find the usages/dependencies connected with these relationships.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Show results in the new tab</strong></td><td><p>Select this check box to display the results of each search in a new tab.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Show this dialog before </strong><strong>searching</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select this check box to open the <strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong> dialog before a new search is started. If you leave the check box empty, the dialog will not open, and the next search will use the search options specified during your last search.</p><p><span>To open the </span><strong>Usages/Dependencies Search Options</strong><span> dialog, c</span>lick <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_dependencies_search_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar of the usages/dependencies search results window.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f1e7632-c154-4db2-8a34-5feff71e891a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1389154634</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676291 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01209: Usages and dependencies search results window

- page_id: `169676291`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676291/Usages+and+dependencies+search+results+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-07-28T09:30:40.126+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Analyzing usages and dependencies
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1387766720

INLINE

100.00002%

1387766722

INLINE

1387766721

INLINE

The usages/dependencies search results window lists the usages/dependencies of the element for which you perform the search. In the usages/dependencies window you can analyze, group and filter search results, find element locations, and more.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The dependencies search results window.

The following table describes how you can use the commands of the usages/dependencies search results dialog toolbar to manage usages/dependencies search results.

| Toolbar element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Expand All | Expands all search results listed in groups. To expand the content of a single group, click the plus sign next to the group name. |
| Collapse All | Collapses all search results listed in groups. To collapse the content of a single group, click the minus sign next to the group name. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Selects the desired element in the containment tree. To enable the button, select one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window. |
| Open Specification | Opens the Specification window of the desired element. To enable the button, select one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window. |
| Add to Search Results | Moves all usages/dependencies search results to the Search Results tab of the model browser. In the Search Results tab, all results are organized in two groups: Found in loaded diagrams and Found in model. |
| Open all diagrams that contain current Usages/Dependencies | Opens all diagrams that use at least one the usages/dependencies search results. The button is inactive when there are no search results used in diagrams. |
| Display the full path of the elements | Displays full paths to the elements next to their names in the usages/dependencies search results window. |
| Group by children | Groups all usages/dependencies search results by children. |
| Refresh | Refreshes usages/dependencies search results if elements or symbols are deleted from the model or new usages/dependencies are created. |
| Usages/Dependencies Search Options | Opens the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog. |
| Print | Prints the usages/dependencies search results table. Make sure that you expand the search results listed in groups if you want them to be shown on the printed page. |
| Export | Exports usages/dependencies search results to a .html or .csv file. |
| Filter | The filter boxes above each column of the usages/dependencies search results table allow you to filter search results by element type, used/dependent element name and usage type. Click a filter box to select the desired filter option. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The usage search results window

The following table describes usages search results window.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Results | Displays results with found usage. |
| Type | Displays element type in which usage was found. |
| Used element | Displays used elements. |
| Used as | Displays usage type. |
| Project | Displays the name of the used project file that owns the model element, which the package/model element uses. |

1387766719

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbb18397-0917-4b4c-8db1-469cc9bde133"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1387766720</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e67ff3e-0a41-49fe-8929-985d7aaab7ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1387766722</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e375b55-08f7-4448-85c6-4488d9578688"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1387766721</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The usages/dependencies search results window lists the usages/dependencies of the element for which you perform the search. In the usages/dependencies window you can analyze, group and filter search results, find element locations, and more.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_dependencies_search_results_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The dependencies search results window.</h6><p>The following table describes how you can use the commands of the usages/dependencies search results dialog toolbar to manage usages/dependencies search results.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 27.7645%;" /><col style="width: 72.2355%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Toolbar element</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_all.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> Expand All</p></div></td><td><p>Expands all search results listed in groups. To expand the content of a single group, click the plus sign next to the group name.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collapse_all.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Collapse All</p></div></td><td>Collapses all s<span>earch results listed in groups</span>. <span>To collapse the content of a single group, click the minus sign next to the group name.</span></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Select in Containment Tree</p></div></td><td><p>Selects the desired element in the containment tree. To enable the button, select one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Open Specification</p></div></td><td>Opens the Specification window of the desired element. To enable the button, s<span>elect one of the search results in the usages/dependencies search result window.</span></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_to_search_results.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Add to Search Results</p></div></td><td><p>Moves all usages/dependencies search results to the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the model browser. In the<span> </span><strong>Search Results</strong><span> tab</span>, all results are organized in two groups: <strong>Found in loaded diagrams</strong> and <strong>Found in model</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_all_diagrams_that_contain_current-usages_dependencies.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Open all diagrams that contain current Usages/Dependencies</p></div></td><td><p>Opens all diagrams that use at least one the usages/dependencies search results. The button is inactive when there are no search results used in diagrams.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Display the full path of the elements</p></div></td><td><p>Displays full paths to the elements next to their names in the usages/dependencies search results window.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="group_by_children.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Group by children</p></div></td><td>Groups all usages/dependencies search results by children.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Refresh</p></div></td><td><p>Refreshes usages/dependencies search results if elements or symbols are deleted from the model or new usages/dependencies are created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_dependencies_search_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Usages/Dependencies Search Options</p></div></td><td>Opens the Usages/Dependencies Search Options dialog.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="print.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Print</p></div></td><td>Prints the usages/dependencies search results table. Make sure that you expand the search results listed in groups if you want them to be shown on the printed page.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> Export</p></div></td><td>Exports usages/dependencies search results to a .html or .csv file.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Filter</td><td colspan="1"><p>The filter boxes above each column of the usages/dependencies search results table allow you to filter search results by element type, used/dependent element name and usage type. Click a filter box to select the desired filter option.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_search_result_window.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usages and dependencies search results window" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The usage search results window</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The following table describes usages search results window.<br /></span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Results</strong></td><td><p>Displays results with found usage.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>Displays element type in which usage was found.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Used element</strong></td><td colspan="1">Displays used elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Used as</strong></td><td>Displays usage type.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Project</strong></td><td colspan="1"><p>Displays the name of the used project file that owns the model element, which the package/model element uses.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f6a275c-044f-4358-9fe3-75e5dc3c688a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1387766719</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675493 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01210: Use Case

- page_id: `169675493`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675493/Use+Case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T10:27:30.503+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: ['use-case', 'use-cases', 'rake-icon', 'actors']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

19622935

19622937

19622936

INLINE

Use Cases are a means of specifying the required usages of a system. Typically, they are used to capture the requirements of a system, that is, what a system is supposed to do.

In the following figure you can see a fragment of the Library System. The Use Case *Register Return* is associated to the Actor *Librarian*, which means that this Actor initiates that Use Case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Behavior of a Use Case can be specified by an Activity, State Machine, or Sequence. It can also be described by a textual description - a Use Case scenario that can be depicted in an Activity diagram.

A Use Case can be specified by changing its property values in the Use Case [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024xR1']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

##### Use Case relationships

A Use Case can be associated with an Actor to specify that the Actor initiates the associated Use Case.

A Use Case can be related to other Use Cases by

- Generalization
- Include
- Extend

##### Use Case shape with rake icon

The rake icon [IMAGE alt='' src=''] is displayed on the shape, if the Use Case is realized by other behavior diagrams, such as Use Case, Activity, State Machine, or Sequence. To hide the rake icon you need to set the **Show Rake Icon** property value to *false* in the **Symbol Property** dialog.

##### Use Case Actors

An Actor can be related to the Use Case by Association.

You can see the Actors that are related to the selected Use Case in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] of that Use Case, the Actors property group. This group displays a list of Actors that are directly (through the Association relationship) or indirectly (through the Extend, Include, or Generalization relationships) connected to the selected Use Case.

19622934

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675503 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01211: Use Case description profile

- page_id: `169675503`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675503/Use+Case+description+profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:21:58.061+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

21188256

21188259

21188258

INLINE

You can extend the Use Case description with additional Use Case properties, such as Use Case ID, Author, Date, Use Case Complexity, Pre Condition, and others.

To extend the Use Case with additional properties, you must load the Use Case Description Profile manually; in a new project, it is not loaded by default.

To load the Use Case Description Profile

1. Open the Use Case [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] .
2. Click the **Load Profile**button. The Use Case Description Profile is loaded and additional properties are added to the Use Case.

*[IMAGE alt='' src='']*

After the Use Case Description Profile is loaded, the following additional properties appear in the Use Case [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']:

- The Use Case ID property in the UseCase general property group. This property is designed to number use cases to correspond to particular sequences.
- The **Use Case Description**property group. In this property group, you can specify properties describing the particular Use Case, such as an Author, Date, Goal, and other.
- The Use Case Scenarios Diagrams property group allows specifying diagrams for basic, alternative, and exceptional Use Case scenarios.
- The **Use Case Scenarios Flows**property group allows specifying flows for basic, alternative, and exceptional Use Case scenarios.

The following figure illustrates an example of the **Use Case** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'] after the Use Case Description Profile is loaded.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can specify the Use Case extension properties in the Use Case [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1']. Descriptions of each property appear in the description area of the Specification window.

21188255

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673866 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01212: Use Case diagram

- page_id: `169673866`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673866/Use+Case+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-01-27T11:24:08.831+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307575757

307575759

307575758

**Overview**

A Use Case diagram falls under the behavioral diagramming family.A Use Case diagram models the users' expectations for using the system. The people and systems that interact with the target system are called *actors*, whereas the features of the system used by actors are called *Use Cases*.

In general, a Use Case is a description of the functionality (a specific usage of a system) that a system provides. They can be written in many different ways, but the most common one is to represent a view of the system from outside the system. The Use Case descriptions can exist in a textual form (a simple table), where the Use Case diagram provides additional information about the relationship between the use cases and the external users. The Use Case diagram also allows a definition of the system's boundary.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1'] are described only in terms of how they appear when viewed externally by the user (a system's behavior as the user perceives it), and do not describe how the functionality is provided inside the system. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024xR1'] are not object-oriented, but they are included in the UML to simplify the approach of the project's lifecycle – from specification to implementation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Purpose**

A Use Case diagram defines the expectations of the external actors, for example, people, systems, or devices that need to interact with the system. Their interactions may be to provide input, to receive output, or to dialog with the system in order to cooperate in the completion of a task. All these interactions are focused using specific features of the system - *Use Cases*. It should be noted that Use Case diagrams do not provide any details about the implementation of these features. The goal of the Use Case approach is to focus the development effort on the essential objectives of the system without getting lost in, or driven by, particular implementations or practices.

**Usage**

Use Case diagrams, together with use case narratives and scenarios, define the goals of a system, or another classifier, such as an enterprise, subsystem, or component.

A Use Case diagram is ideal to:

- represent the goals of system-user interactions
- define and organize functional requirements in a system
- identify internal and external factors that may influence the system and should be taken into consideration
- specify the context and requirements of a system
- model the basic flow of events in a Use Case.

**Summary**

Use Case diagrams are valuable because they:

- identify the client's expectations for the system
- identify specific features of the system
- identify shared behavior among system features
- allows clients to view their requirements in a simple and easy way.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Use Case diagram

307575756

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f63b840-b9f5-48da-84b9-0b024d8cc1a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307575757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd707cbf-aff9-4019-9c2e-0dc898dd4ff3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307575759</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4201b6b8-f796-449a-8505-c94e032be31e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307575758</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Overview</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A Use Case diagram falls under the behavioral diagramming family. </span>A Use Case diagram models the users' expectations for using the system. The people and systems that interact with the target system are called <em>actors</em>, whereas the features of the system used by actors are called <em>Use Cases</em>.</p><p>In general, a Use Case is a description of the functionality (a specific usage of a system) that a system provides. They can be written in many different ways, but the most common one is to represent a view of the system from outside the system. The Use Case descriptions can exist in a textual form (a simple table), where the Use Case diagram provides additional information about the relationship between the use cases and the external users. The Use Case diagram also allows a definition of the system's boundary.</p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are described only in terms of how they appear when viewed externally by the user (a system's behavior as the user perceives it), and do not describe how the functionality is provided inside the system. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are not object-oriented, but they are included in the UML to simplify the approach of the project's lifecycle – from specification to implementation.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The schematic view of the use cases in the system" ac:alt="The schematic view of the use cases in the system"><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_cases_system.gif"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A Use Case diagram defines the expectations of the external actors, for example, people, systems, or devices that need to interact with the system. Their interactions may be to provide input, to receive output, or to dialog with the system in order to cooperate in the completion of a task. All these interactions are focused using specific features of the system - <em>Use Cases</em>. It should be noted that Use Case diagrams do not provide any details about the implementation of these features. The goal of the Use Case approach is to focus the development effort on the essential objectives of the system without getting lost in, or driven by, particular implementations or practices. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Use Case diagrams, together with use case narratives and scenarios, define the goals of a system, or another classifier, such as an enterprise, subsystem, or component. </p><p>A Use Case diagram is ideal to:</p><ul><li>represent the goals of system-user interactions</li><li>define and organize functional requirements in a system</li><li>identify internal and external factors that may influence the system and should be taken into consideration</li><li>specify the context and requirements of a system</li><li>model the basic flow of events in a Use Case.</li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Use Case diagrams are valuable because they: </p><ul><li>identify the client's expectations for the system</li><li>identify specific features of the system</li><li>identify shared behavior among system features</li><li>allows clients to view their requirements in a simple and easy way. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="use_case_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Use Case diagram<br /><br /></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56022e7e-0dde-4bac-8862-50030959494e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307575756</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 01213: Use Case diagram elements

- page_id: `169673443`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

490104335

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

490104338

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

490104336

This page describes all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1'] elements.

##### Actor with Properties

An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024xR1']with properties will be transformed into a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1']with the «EAActor»****stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An actor with properties.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An Actor that has been converted to a Class with «EAActor» will not display some properties (such as Fill Color) because the stereotype image will be shown instead.

##### Use Case with Invalid Inner Elements

A NestedClassifier, ownedComment, ownedRule, ownedAttribute, or ownedOperation cannot be an inner element of a uml:UseCase. It will be moved to a new created realized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024xR1'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Use Case with invalid inner elements.

##### Boundary

A boundary in EA will be converted into a rectangle with rounded corners. The boundary can contain inner elements. Unlike the rectangular boundary, the boundary in EA will take all inner elements with it whenever it is moved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Boundaries.

490104334

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678578 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01214: Use Case driven

- page_id: `169678578`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678578/Use+Case+driven
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T14:50:53.488+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1337564773

1337564774

1337564774

Report Wizard provides a set of templates to support the Use Case Driven approach methodology. A project that creates the Use Case Driven approach development steps can generate a report to capture elements.

Report Wizard provides 3 templates for the Use Case Driven approach:

1. Use Case Specification Report
2. Method Specification Report
3. Use Case Project Estimation Report

You can create a new project using the Use Case Driven approach either as:

(i) A blank project with the *UseCase_Profile.xml* module or 
(ii) A Use Case project

To create a new project as a blank project using *UseCase_Profile.xml*

1. On the main menu, click File .
2. Click Use Module . The Use Module dialog will open.
3. Select the UseCase_Profile.xml module.
4. Click Finish . The project will apply the Use Case Description profile.

To create a new project as a Use Case Project

- A project will apply the Use Case Description profile automatically.

1337564773

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678581 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01215: Use Case project estimation report

- page_id: `169678581`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678581/Use+Case+project+estimation+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-24T11:51:12.767+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Use Case driven
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The use case project estimation or 'Use Case Points' is a method for sizing and estimating projects developed with the object-oriented method, developed by Gustav Karner of Objectory (now Rational Software). The method is an extension of Function Point Analysis and Mk II Function Point Analysis (an adaption of FPA mainly used in the UK). 
The Use Case Project Estimation report is intended to estimate the project size and duration of hourly manpower required based on the use case model. The report supports use case and SysML use case diagrams.

The use case point technique is applied to reestimate the project size. All the use cases used in the example below are based on the use case diagram. The following weight criteria are used:

#### Classifying Actors

The table below lists Actors Classifications

| Actor Complexity | Litmus Test to recognize classifications | Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Low Complexity | An external system with a well-defined API. | 1 |
| Average complexity | Either a human with a command line interface or an external system via some protocols or a database. | 2 |
| High complexity | A human with a GUI or a web page. | 3 |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification of Actor.

#### Unadjusted Actor Weights

Unadjusted Actor Weights (UAW) is obtained by counting how many actors there are in each category, multiplying each total by its weight, and adding up the products.

#### Determining Scenarios and Transactions of Use Cases

The table below lists Use Case Complexity

| Use Case Complexity | Litmus Test To Decide Classifications | Weight |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Low complexity | 1 - 3 transactions | 5 |
| Average complexity | 4 - 7 transactions | 10 |
| High complexity | > 7 transactions | 15 |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven - Use Case Complexity.

#### Unadjusted Use Case Weights

A transaction is a set of activities, which is either performed entirely or not at all. To determine the number of transactions, you need to count the use case steps. Next, multiply each use case type by the weighting factor and add up the products to get **Unadjusted Use Case Weights (UUCW)**.

#### Unadjusted Use Case Point

Add UAW and UUCW to get Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP): UUCP = UAW + UUCW.

Reference Documents

- The Estimation of Effort Based on Use Cases, published by Rational software [ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf](ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf)
- COCOMO II [http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html](http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The use case project estimation or 'Use Case Points' is a method for sizing and estimating projects developed with the object-oriented method, developed by Gustav Karner of Objectory (now Rational Software). The method is an extension of Function Point Analysis and Mk II Function Point Analysis (an adaption of FPA mainly used in the UK).<br />The Use Case Project Estimation report is intended to estimate the project size and duration of hourly manpower required based on the use case model. The report supports use case and SysML use case diagrams.</p><p>The use case point technique is applied to reestimate the project size. All the use cases used in the example below are based on the use case diagram. The following weight criteria are used:</p><h2>Classifying Actors</h2><p>The table below lists Actors Classifications</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 50.1275%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.8113%;" /><col style="width: 55.2647%;" /><col style="width: 16.0245%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Actor Complexity</th><th>Litmus Test to recognize classifications</th><th>Weight</th></tr><tr><td>Low Complexity</td><td>An external system with a well-defined <span>API.</span></td><td>1</td></tr><tr><td>Average complexity</td><td>Either a human with a command line interface <br />or an external system via some protocols or a <br />database.</td><td>2</td></tr><tr><td>High complexity</td><td>A human with a GUI or a web page.</td><td>3</td></tr></tbody></table><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image ac:title="Specification of Actor" ac:alt="Specification of Actor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actor-complexity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case project estimation report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Specification of Actor.</h6><h2>Unadjusted Actor Weights</h2><p>Unadjusted Actor Weights (UAW) is obtained by counting how many actors there are in each category, multiplying each total by its weight, and adding up the products.</p><h2>Determining Scenarios and Transactions of Use Cases</h2><p>The table below lists Use Case Complexity</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 50.2011%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 37.719%;" /><col style="width: 46.2335%;" /><col style="width: 16.1478%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Use Case Complexity</th><th>Litmus Test To Decide Classifications</th><th>Weight</th></tr><tr><td>Low complexity</td><td>1 - 3 transactions</td><td>5</td></tr><tr><td>Average complexity</td><td>4 - 7 transactions</td><td>10</td></tr><tr><td><p>High complexity</p></td><td> &gt; 7 transactions</td><td>15</td></tr></tbody></table><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Use Case Driven - Use Case Complexity" ac:alt="Use Case Driven - Use Case Complexity"><ri:attachment ri:filename="use-case-complexity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Use Case project estimation report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Use Case Driven - Use Case Complexity.</h6><h2>Unadjusted Use Case Weights</h2><p>A transaction is a set of activities, which is either performed entirely or not at all. To determine the number of transactions, you need to count the use case steps. Next, multiply each use case type by the weighting factor and add up the products to get <strong>Unadjusted Use Case Weights (UUCW)</strong>.</p><h2>Unadjusted Use Case Point</h2><p>Add UAW and UUCW to get Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP): UUCP = UAW + UUCW.</p><p>Reference Documents</p><ul><li>The Estimation of Effort Based on Use Cases, published by Rational software<br />            <em> <a href="ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf" style="line-height: 1.4285715;">ftp://ftp.software.ibm.com/software/rational/web/whitepapers/2003/finalTP171.pdf</a></em></li><li>COCOMO II<br />             <em><a href="http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html" style="line-height: 1.4285715;">http://sunset.usc.edu/csse/research/COCOMOII/cocomo_main.html</a></em></li></ul>
````
