<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676432 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01241: Using Simple Navigation

- page_id: `169676432`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676432/Using+Simple+Navigation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:21.490+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

70623058

70623070

70631049

**On this page:**

****

70623060

Use the Simple Navigation operation type if you need to specify a direct relation between the elements through model relationships, properties, tags, or UML operations.

Please note that the Simple Navigation operation returns a set.

##### Specifying a Simple Navigation operation as criteria

To specify a Simple Navigation operation as criteria

1. Click Simple Navigation on the left side of the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='simple_navigation_1.png']
2. Specify the Relation Criterion .
3. Specify Is Applied , Direction . [ATTACHMENT filename='simple_navigation_2.png']

In the table below you can see the options that can be specified for Simple Navigation.

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Relation Criterion | Name of a relationship, property, tag, or UML meta-operation. The whole list is by default filtered and shows only these relationships, properties, tags, and operations that are available for the type. To see the full list, click to clear the check box below the list. |
| Is Applied | Select the check box to define the appropriate relationship, property, tag, or UML operation as criteria for the calculation. To clear all the selected criteria, click the Clear All button. |
| Direction | Select from the drop-down list the direction of the appropriate relationship, property, tag, or UML operation.A source is a .A target is the result of the calculation.If the direction is defined for relationships, thenThe Source To Target direction means that only the outgoing relations, which are pointing from the source element to the target element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.The Target To Source direction means that only the incoming relations, which are pointing from the target element to the source element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.If Both direction is chosen, both above-described cases will be treated as a valid result.If the direction is defined for properties or tags, thenThe Source To Target direction means that only the properties, which exist in the source element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.The Target To Source direction means that only the properties, which exist in the target element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.If Both direction is chosen, both above-described cases will be treated as a valid result.Operations support the Source To Target direction only. |
| Style | Click the button and in the open dialog select the color and style to represent the appropriate relationship, property, tag, or UML operation. The column is available only in the Dependency Criteria and Relation Criteria dialogs. |
| Properties | Click the button and in the open dialog specify the properties of the selected relationship:Include Relationship Subtypes - select the check box to include the subtypes of the selected relationship types.Include Relationship Custom Types - select the check box to include the custom relationship types that extend the selected relationship types.Filter by Property Value - click the button and in the open dialog select one or more properties of the selected relationship and specify their values for a more specific filter. |
| Result Type | Click the button and in the open Select Element Type dialog select one or more result element types. The Result Type column is available only in the Query and Expression dialogs. You can define the element types for representation on a Dependency Matrix and Generic table. Moreover, you can define the element types for representation on a Relation Map. |
| Unique | Select the check box to have only one identical element count as the result.Note: the option is unavailable if Simple Navigation is nested directly under the root union. In such a case, the root union controls the uniqueness.The option is especially important to return non-unique numeric values to correctly execute mathematical operations (such as Sum). |

##### Advanced Manipulations

###### Changing the Contextual element of a Simple Navigation criterion

To change the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1'] of a Simple Navigation criterion

1. On the left side of the dialog, expand the Simple Navigation criterion and select the Context parameter.
2. On the Context specification panel, click the Reset button. [ATTACHMENT filename='simple_navigation_4.png'] The value of the Context parameter becomes null.
3. Select a new context from the list of operations and element types. To restore the original context, expand the **Other** group and click **Contextual Variable**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the expression of a Simple Navigation operation as a parameter of another operation

To use the expression of a Simple Navigation operation as a parameter of another operation

1. On the left side of the dialog, click Simple Navigation .
2. On the criteria specification panel, click the **Use** **as** button and select the appropriate command. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Specify other parameters of the new operation.

70623057

    - Calling operations from the model
    - Creating script operations

element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br />A target is the result of the calculation.</p><p>If the direction is defined for relationships, then</p><ul><li>The <strong>Source To Target</strong> direction means that only the outgoing relations, which are pointing from the source element to the target element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.</li><li>The <strong>Target To Source</strong> direction means that only the incoming relations, which are pointing from the target element to the source element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.</li><li>If <strong>Both</strong> direction is chosen, both above-described cases will be treated as a valid result.</li></ul><p>If the direction is defined for properties or tags, then</p><ul><li>The <strong>Source To Target</strong> direction means that only the properties, which exist in the source element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.</li><li>The <strong>Target To Source</strong> direction means that only the properties, which exist in the target element, will be treated as a result of this criterion.</li><li>If <strong>Both</strong> direction is chosen, both above-described cases will be treated as a valid result.</li></ul><p>Operations support the <strong>Source To Target</strong> direction only.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Style</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and in the open dialog select the color and style to represent the appropriate relationship, property, tag, or UML operation. The column is available only in the <strong>Dependency Criteria</strong> and </span><strong>Relation Criteria</strong><span> dialogs.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Properties</span></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> button and in the open dialog specify the properties of the selected relationship:</p><ul><li><span><strong>Include Relationship Subtypes</strong> - </span>select the check box to include the subtypes of the selected relationship types.</li><li><span><strong>Include Relationship Custom Types</strong> - select the check box to include the custom relationship types that extend the selected relationship types.</span></li><li><strong>Filter by Property Value</strong> - c<span>lick the </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span> button and in the open dialog select one or more properties of the selected relationship and specify their values for a more specific filter.</span></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Result Type</span></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Click the </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span> button and in the open </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Selecting+element+types"><strong>Select Element Type</strong></a><span> dialog select one or more result element types. The Result Type column is available only in the </span><strong>Query</strong><span> and </span><strong>Expression</strong><span> dialogs. You can define the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Selecting+criteria">element types</a><span> for representation on a Dependency Matrix and Generic table. Moreover, you can define the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specifying+criteria%2C+layout%2C+and+depth">element types</a><span> for representation on a Relation Map.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Unique</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span><span>Select the check box to have only one identical element count as the result.</span></span></p><p><span><span><strong>Note</strong>: the<span> option is unavailable if Simple Navigation is nested directly under the root union. In such a case, the root union controls the uniqueness.<br /></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d5690b9-e0f3-4bda-99ce-0a3349591cce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The option is especially important to return non-unique numeric values to correctly execute mathematical operations (such as Sum).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Advanced Manipulations</h3><h4>Changing the Contextual element of a Simple Navigation criterion</h4><p>To change the <ac:link ac:anchor="Contextual Element"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a Simple Navigation criterion</p><hr /><ol><li>On the left side of the dialog, expand the Simple Navigation criterion and select the <strong>Context</strong> parameter.</li><li>On the <strong>Context</strong> specification panel, click the <strong>Reset</strong> button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simple_navigation_4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The value of the <strong>Context</strong> parameter becomes null. </li><li><p>Select a new context from the list of operations and element types.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3f6a791-ffbf-4f7b-b8d8-27ef36d328af"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the original context, expand the <strong>Other</strong> group and click <strong>Contextual Variable</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="contextual_variable.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Using the expression of a Simple Navigation operation as a parameter of another operation</h4><p>To use the expression of a Simple Navigation operation as a parameter of another operation</p><hr /><ol><li>On the left side of the dialog, click <strong>Simple Navigation</strong>.</li><li><p>On the criteria specification panel, click the <strong>Use</strong> <strong>as</strong> button and select the appropriate command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simple_navigation_use_as.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Simple Navigation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></li><li>Specify other parameters of the new operation.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6231b7e6-b088-4027-be7c-e46263736a25"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">70623057</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ac:link></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Specifying+criteria+for+querying+model">Specifying criteria for querying model</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Getting+started+with+specifying+criteria">Getting started with specifying criteria</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Metachain+Navigation">Using Metachain Navigation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Find+operation">Using Find operation</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Using+Implied+Relations">Using Implied Relations</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+new+operations">Creating new operations</a><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Calling+operations+from+the+model">Calling operations from the model</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Creating+script+operations">Creating script operations</a></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169675391 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01242: Using smart packages in your model

- page_id: `169675391`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169675391/Using+smart+packages+in+your+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:10.255+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1701234985

1701234988

1701234987

To better understand the usability of the smart packages, please see the following case studies.

##### Case study #1: Gathering use cases

The case study uses the sample project *use case diagram.mdzip*, which can be found in*<modeling tool installation folder>\samples\diagrams*.

For example, we have a lot of use cases owned by different system boundaries in the project.

We need all these use cases to be in a single package, except for several particular ones. Also, we need all newly created use cases to be automatically included in this package.

In this case, the smart packages feature is very useful. We will create a smart package with dynamic contents to gather all use cases in the model, then demonstrate how it manages further changes in the model. Finally, we will create a snapshot of the smart package to have a static list of use cases as a milestone of the model development.

To accomplish this, do the following:

1. Create a smart package named All Use Cases . [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_package_all_use_cases.png']
2. Define the criteria for gathering the contents of the smart package. Specify the search options to find all use case type elements from the root package Data . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_search_options.png']
3. Expand the contents of the smart package All Use Cases . [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_contents_of_smart_package.png']
4. Exclude use cases Change password and Change system settings from the contents. See the contents of the smart package shortened.
5. Open the Specification window of the smart package and see the excluded use cases in the cell of the Excluded Elements property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='excluded_elements.png']
6. Drag use cases Change password and Change system settings to the smart package. See them in the contents of the smart package again.
7. Open the Specification window of the smart package again and see these manually included use cases in the cell of the Additional Elements property value. Elements excluded from the contents appear in it after being manually added. [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_elements.png']
8. Create a new use case named Filter by author under the system boundary Item Browser . See the new use case in the smart package All Use Cases . [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_by_author.png']
9. Create a snapshot of the smart package All Use Cases to have a static list of use cases as a milestone of the model development. [ATTACHMENT filename='snapshot_smart_package.png']
10. Expand the contents of the snapshot and see that it equals the contents of the smart package All Use Cases .

##### Case study #2: Performing the requirements coverage analysis

The case study uses the sample project *hybrid sport utility vehicle.mdzip*, which can be found in *<modeling tool installation folder>\samples\SysML*, if the SysML plugin is installed.

Here is another case where the smart package feature is very helpful: we need to have all unsatisfied requirements in a separate package. Also, we need requirements to automatically disappear from this package after becoming satisfied.

We will create a smart package with dynamic contents to gather all the unsatisfied requirements in the model and a dependency matrix for performing the requirements coverage analysis. Then we will demonstrate how both the smart package and the dependency matrix reflect the transition of a requirement to satisfy.

Let’s do the following:

1. Create a smart package named Unsatisfied Requirements .
2. To gather the contents of the smart package, add a new script operation (in the **Expert** mode of the **Query** dialog) and define the following OCL 2.0 expression as the criteria: select(r|not r.supplierDependency->exists(d|d.oclIsKindOf(SysML::Satisfy)))]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Expand the contents of the smart package Unsatisfied Requirements . [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_package_unsatisfied_requirements.png']
4. Create a dependency matrix and define the following criteria:
  - Specify Requirement as the row element type
  - Specify Block as the column element type
  - Specify the smart package Unsatisfied Requirements as the row scope
  - Specify the package HSUV Structure as the column scope
  - Specify the Satisfy relationship as dependency criteria
  - In the Direction drop-down list, select Column to row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. On the dependency matrix, create a Satisfy relationship between the block BrakePedal and the requirement Braking . [ATTACHMENT filename='SP_create_satisfy.png'] The requirement becomes satisfied and thus disappears from the contents of the smart package Unsatisfied Requirements and from the dependency matrix as well.

##### Case Study #3: Configuration management of the complex system - creating dynamic configuration catalogs

*The efficient configuration management process is a challenge in the evolution of any industrial scale product family. Smart packages are a real-life out of the box solution supporting the configuration management approach.*

Now study the case that illustrates the efficient management of complex system configurations with the help of smart packages. We have a library (static package) of system components, which we need to see in several different views of the model, that is, catalogs, according to their characteristics. Using the catalogs will not extend the scope of the model, since they do not require duplication of the elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

We will create two dynamic system configuration catalogs, that is, smart packages with dynamic contents, to gather the servers from the library *TI Hardware* according to the configuration version defined in a tag value of their specification.

Do the following:

1. Create two packages: TI12 Catalog and TI14 Catalog .
2. In each package, create a smart package named Servers . [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_package_servers.png']
3. Define criteria for gathering the contents of the smart package Servers in TI12 Catalog . Specify search options to find in the package TI Hardware , all block type elements with tag value Used In=TI12. [ATTACHMENT filename='search_smart_package_step3.png']
4. Define criteria for gathering the contents of the smart package Servers in TI14 Catalog. Specify search options to find in the package TI Hardware, all block type elements with tag value Used In=TI14 . [ATTACHMENT filename='search_smart_package_step4.png']
5. Expand the contents of both smart packages. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The block *SUN FIRE T1000* appears in both *TI12 Catalog* and *TI14 Catalog*, since it has both tag values *Used In=TI12* and *Used In=TI14*.
6. Also, you can add a block to a smart package manually. Just drag the block to the smart package.

1701234982

**Related Pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024xR1']

- Stereotype
- Package

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169679126 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01243: Using Standard UML Properties

- page_id: `169679126`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169679126/Using+Standard+UML+Properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-08-16T11:49:48.623+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Customizing Specification Window
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

DSL element specification may contain custom properties defined in a stereotype.All DSL element properties are used by default, but you may select which UML properties should be used in the DSL element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR1'].

To use standard UML properties

1. Open the customization element Specification window.
2. Click the Used UML Properties property value specification cell and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button.
3. In the Select properties dialog, set the selected standard UML properties that you want to be displayed in the DSL element Specification window value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Select Properties dialog.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Specification Window' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Always Visible Properties' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Meta property substitution (changing name of UML property)' space='MD2024xR1']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677128 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01244: Using Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `169677128`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677128/Using+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:25.044+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1170281217

1170281219

1170281218

##### Introduction

Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) is No Magic’s next-generation repository for collaborative development and versioned storage of models.TWCloud is intended and developed to improve the areas of model governance, model analysis, and integration with third-party tools. The benefits to enterprise users include:

- Role-Based Access Control is a major feature that enables efficient management of users’ access rights across the enterprise. Additionally, as distributed systems can be challenging to administer, we have built a convenient [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud Admin' space='TWCloud2024xR1'] , which provides a single way to manage user accounts, licensing, access control, LDAP integration, and SSL setup .
- TWCloud is designed from the ground up to support working with large models. In particular, the efficient delta-based protocol used when users commit and retrieve updates to a model will save time for those users who access the repository through poor network connections. Likewise, the amount of data that is transmitted depends only on the size of the changes being made to the model .
- A database-driven model storage paradigm in TWCloud unveils powerful client-side features for model changes and history tracking at element level.

- Project Usages location : rather than mounting everything under the main model root element, the Model Browser has a dedicated root for Project Usages in Teamwork Cloud.
- Shared Packages : there are no shared packages in the TWCloud Project Usages; all content is available for reference. However, the TWCloud project can be stripped using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding packages' space='MD2024xR1'] feature.
- The mounting of Used Projects in TWCloud is achieved via a model-based Mount relationship. In order to ensure the same Containment tree structure when adding/migrating projects to TWCloud, original mounting is re-created by adding the Mount relationship automatically.
- Teamwork Cloud architecture supports the sticky versions (used project versions that remain used until they are changed in the main project) approach of used projects. However, you can easily update the used project to the latest version by setting up a notification that will inform you about the appearance of a new used project version. Alternatively, you can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Used Projects Auto Update Plugin' space='MD2024xR1'] that allows the automatic update of used projects.
- In Teamwork Cloud, every new version of the project gets a unique consecutive number, even between different branches, which makes it easier to keep track of the commit history and identify the specific project versions.
- Teamwork Cloud used [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024xR1']. This helps to prevent cyclic dependencies. We recommend that you open a used project as a stand-alone and edit it there; however, you can move elements to other projects using the [cross-project refactoring functionality](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Moving+elements+to+other+projects) in Teamwork Cloud as well. This feature allows transferring data between projects in just a few steps.

You can find more information about the TWCloud's key benefits, required resources, and the latest news on its official product page here [http://www.nomagic.com/products/teamwork-cloud.html](http://www.nomagic.com/products/teamwork-cloud.html).

1170281216

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ad2f539-e088-4b8b-82bc-ab790e9fcc93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1170281217</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9508902-3895-4343-be4e-d49c765829b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1170281219</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="152dbd63-423a-46e0-a6e5-42c5f3acd566"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1170281218</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) is No Magic’s next-generation repository for collaborative development and versioned storage of models.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span>TWCloud is intended and developed <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9af061bc-2793-45d9-8c06-f5e827216e3f">to improve</ac:inline-comment-marker> the areas of model governance, model analysis, and integration with third-party tools. The benefits to enterprise <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8a7ed901-75c2-45a1-a0df-deceb6eb2add">users include</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</p><ul><li>Role-Based Access Control is a major feature that enables efficient management of users’ access rights across the enterprise. Additionally, as distributed systems can be challenging to administer, we have built a convenient <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3741cee0-d56b-483c-9cd3-45600761e173"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud Admin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[web-based administrative interface to TWCloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></ac:inline-comment-marker>, which provides a single way to manage user accounts, licensing, access control, LDAP integration, and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e7035c4-7826-414d-abb2-e549a57bbeba">SSL setup</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>TWCloud is designed from the ground up to support working with large models. In particular, the efficient delta-based protocol used when users commit and retrieve updates to a model will save time for those users who access the repository through poor network connections. Likewise, the amount of data that is transmitted depends only on the size of the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d3b084a1-895e-4d42-ae95-47e17d8bb46a">changes</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bc1301d0-d3be-41c8-936d-012767dacc88">being made to the model</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A database-driven model storage paradigm in TWCloud unveils powerful client-side features for model changes and history tracking at element level.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ul><ul><li><strong>Project Usages location</strong>: rather than mounting everything under the main model root element, the Model Browser has a dedicated root for Project Usages in Teamwork Cloud.</li><li><strong>Shared Packages</strong>: there are no shared packages in the TWCloud Project Usages; all content is available for reference. However, the TWCloud project can be stripped using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Hiding packages" /></ac:link> feature. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The mounting of Used Projects in TWCloud is achieved via a model-based Mount relationship. In order to ensure the same Containment tree structure when adding/migrating projects to TWCloud, original mounting is re-created by adding the Mount relationship automatically.</span></li><li>Teamwork Cloud architecture supports the sticky versions (used project versions that remain used until they are changed in the main project) approach of used projects. However, you can easily update the used project to the latest version by setting up a notification that will inform you about the appearance of a new used project version. Alternatively, you can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Used Projects Auto Update Plugin" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud Used Project Updater plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that allows the automatic update of used projects. </li><li><p>In Teamwork Cloud, every new version of the project gets a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ee998a4-11f0-4386-a2e4-87d78137c299">unique consecutive number</ac:inline-comment-marker>, even between different branches, which makes it easier to keep track of the commit history and identify the specific project versions. </p></li><li><p>Teamwork Cloud used <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[projects are read-only]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This helps to prevent cyclic dependencies. We recommend that you open a used project as a stand-alone and edit it there; however, you can move elements to other projects using the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Moving+elements+to+other+projects">cross-project refactoring functionality</a> in Teamwork Cloud as well. This feature allows transferring data between projects in just a few steps.  <br /><br /></p></li></ul><p>You can find more information about the TWCloud's key benefits, required resources, and the latest news on its official product page here <a href="http://www.nomagic.com/products/teamwork-cloud.html" style="text-decoration: underline;" rel="nofollow">http://www.nomagic.com/products/teamwork-cloud.html</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c31457-a276-4ce7-80e3-e06c66e48b21"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1170281216</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d0b7e041-b91c-4c4b-b6de-eca22906aaa1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674436 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01245: Using terms in your model

- page_id: `169674436`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674436/Using+terms+in+your+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-07-11T07:20:04.856+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Glossary table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After terms are defined in the glossary, it is easy to use them in your project.

33

##### Inserting terms

To use terms in your model

1. Press Ctrl + Space while typing.
2. Select the term from the list. The term is inserted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using acronyms

You can define capitalized acronyms, such as ABS or MBSE, in the glossary (see the figure below).

#### INFO: Information

Information

Only capitalized terms are recognized as acronyms. Lowercase words are not interpreted as acronyms.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The option to recognize acronyms everywhere in the text is enabled by default.

To disable acronyms usage

1. From the Glossary Table toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='options.png'] .
2. Click to clear the Use Acronyms check box.

##### Navigation through terms

When a term is a whole phrase and one or more words are also defined as terms, you can easily navigate through all those terms in the description window. We recommend creating a new package where the Glossary Table and all terms will be stored. In the new package, create a Glossary Table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


## PAGE 01246: Using the #forrow directive

- page_id: `169678725`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678725/Using+the+forrow+directive
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-12-26T07:01:16.107+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Report Wizard Directives > #forrow directive
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1. Open Microsoft Word or OpenOffice.org Writer.
2. Create a two-column and two-row table (see figure below). [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavb4c5b2fc58987f51d9e1a5730c034702.png']
3. In the second row, first column type: " #forrow ($uc in $UseCase) $uc.name ", and in the second row, second column type: " $uc.owner.humanName #endrow " (see figure below). [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav69ea4f73930fb9ca4337b3704d159304.png']
4. Save the template, and import it to Report Wizard.
5. Generate a Use Case report. The report will appear as shown in the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavf05f28f0a1ceed8e3d44af4f80c5a72b.png']

The report will appear as shown in the figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="A Generated Report" ac:alt="A Generated Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavf05f28f0a1ceed8e3d44af4f80c5a72b.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Using the #forrow directive" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169678505 space=MD2024xR1 version=3 -->
## PAGE 01247: Using the Generate command to generate reports

- page_id: `169678505`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169678505/Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-01-08T11:13:39.845+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating reports from the command line
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

###### GenerateCommand**Using the Generate command**

You can enter the Generate command in the command line to generate a report.

To generate a report from the package scope

- generate - project projectFileName -output outputFileName -template templateName -package packageNameList

To generate a report from the element scope

- generate -project projectFileName -output outputFileName -template templateName -element elementNameList

To generate a report from the option specified in the properties file

- generate -properties propertiesFileName

To generate an encrypted password for a server project

- generate -generatepassword password

###### Description**Description**

The Generate command creates a report document with a received set of information from arguments and parameters. The information will then be generated as a report document to the specified output file. By default, an argument is the specified data of the invoked parameters. If the -properties option is specified, the argument is the name of a properties file. A properties file contains other parameters, along with the specified data of each parameter.

Synopsis

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -project projectFileName | Specifies the project path as a file name.NoteYou can specify a project name and its branch after -project, for example:Specifying the MyProject name alone means generating MyProject without specifying its branch.Specifying MyProject##release means generating MyProject from the [release] branch.Specifying MyProject##release##sp1 means generating MyProject from the [release] branch and [sp1] sub-branch. |
| -output outputFileName | Specifies the output file path as a file name. |
| -template templateName | Specifies a template name used to generate a report document.NoteThe template must exist in the modeling tool prior to using this command, otherwise, an error will occur.If the template name is not specified, the template specified in the Report Data (specified in the -report parameter) will be used instead. |
| -templatePath templatePath | Specifies a template path used to generate a report document. Both absolute and relative paths are allowed for this command. The specified template file must have proper structure as it has been added through the Report Wizard dialog. The folder (directory) of the template must be defined.NoteIf both -template and -templatePath are specified in the same command, the error will occur. |
| -package packageNameList | Specifies the name of one or more packages in a project to be included in the report. A semicolon must separate multiple package entries (;).Warning: Duplicate qualified nameDo not enter a duplicate name of a root Model package or its immediate child package to the -package option. |
| -element elementQualifiedNameList | Specifies the qualified name of one or more elements from a project to be included in a report. A semicolon must separate element entries (;). |
| -elementID | Specifies the element ID of one or more elements in a project to be included in the report. A semicolon must separate multiple element entries (;).NoteElement ID can be retrieved in:Modeling tool element Specifications dialog under All Properties mode.Velocity Template Language by calling $element.elementID for $element.The element ID will be changed when the local project is added to a server. Please make sure the element ID is up-to-date. |
| [options] | Command-line options. |
| -properties propertiesFileName | Specifies the name of a properties file to utilize. Use only with -properties. |
| -generatepassword password | Generates an encrypted password to be used with a properties file. |

###### Options**Options**

The command line feature supports a set of options useful in adding additional configuration possibilities.

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -report reportDataName\|reportDataElementID | Specifies the name of a file or Report Data element.NoteThe Report Data file must exist in the modeling tool prior to using this command, otherwise an error will occur.The value for this command can be either a Report Data name or Report Data element ID.If the -report parameter is not specified, the previous Report Data will be set and used instead. |
| -autoImage 0\|1\|2\|3 | Specifies how an image will be shown in a report document. The default value for this argument is 1.0 – No resize.1 – Fit image to paper (large only).2 – Fit and rotate image (clockwise) to paper (large only).3 – Fit and rotate image (counter-clockwise) to paper (large only) |
| -imageFormat jpg\|png\|svg\|emf\|wmf | Specifies an image format in a report document. The default value for this argument is jpg.jpg – Joint Photographic Expert Grouppng – Portable Network Graphicssvg – Scaling Vector Imageemf – Microsoft Windows Enhanced Metafilewmf – Windows Metafile |
| -recursive true\|false | Specifies how to select a package in a project. The default value for this argument is true.true – Select the specified package and its recursive package.false – Select only the specified package. |
| -includeAuxiliary true\|false | Selects packages, including auxiliary packages. The default value is false. |
| -outputOnBlankField stringValue | Shows a string value in a blank field in a report document. The default value for this argument is “”. |
| -category categoryName | Specifies a template category. |
| -fields[name=value] | Specifies a variable. |
| -spassword encryptedPassword | Specifies an encrypted password to log on to the server. This option is available only in a properties file.You can get encrypted password by entering this command below in command line prompt.text |
| -upload serverAddress | Specifies a serverAddress consisting of a scheme, userInfo, host, port, and path to connect and upload a generated report to a server (see ).NoteIn case the server has not been trusted, a message will be displayed in the command line, asking you to choose to accept or reject the server certificate. |
| -trustAll true\|false | Trusts any certificate of the specific publishing server in -upload command. The default value is false.If the value is true, the certificate will be trusted without asking any questions. |
| -overwrite true\|false | Overwrites any previously generated files. The default value for this argument is true.true – Overwrites all files automatically (removes any existing file before generating a report.)false – If the file exists, the following prompt message will open:Output file already exists.Would you like to replace the existing file? [y][n]:If you type ‘y’ or ‘yes’, the Report Wizard will generate a new report that replaces the previous one. If you type ‘n’ or ‘no’, the Report Wizard will not generate a report and terminate the process. |
| Output file already exists.Would you like to replace the existing file? [y][n]: |  |
| -pversion projectVersion | Specifies a server project version. |
| -ssl true\|false | Specifies if a Secured Connection (SSL) is used. The default value is false.true - Use Secured Connection (SSL)false - Do not use Secured Connection (SSL) |
| -tag tagValue | An additional argument to generate reports from a server project by specifying a server project tag name. The tagValue is non-case sensitive. More than one project version may be returned from specifying one tag value. Hence, one report generation process may generate multiple reports. You cannot use this command with –pversion. |
| -incremental true\|false | Specifies the incremental method. The default value is true.true - The output report shall be generated by appending a new generated output file name with the incremental number.false - The latest project version in the returned project collection. Use this command with -tag only. |
| -leaveprojectopen | Leaves the project open per property file after generating the report, in a series of report generation. The default value is false. |
| -loadmodule true\|false | Loads all unloaded modules in a project. The default value is false.true - All modules including unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project.false - All modules except unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project. |
| -projectpassword projectPassword | Specifies a password for the protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required to open a protected Teamwork Cloud project for generating a report. |

###### UsingCommand**Using command line**

You can use the command line on Windows, Linux, or Unix.

To use the command line on Windows

1. Run a command line console. In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.
2. Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.

To use the command line on Linux or Unix

1. Run a terminal (command line console). In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.
2. Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.

See the following examples of command line on Windows and Linux or Unix respectively.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### MultipleReportsGenerating multiple reports

You can open multiple projects and generate multiple reports before the modeling tool shuts down. The **generate** command with the -properties option accepts multiple properties files as shown below.

| generate -properties <file1.properties> <file2.properties> <file3.properties>... |
| --- |

For example

| generate -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties” |
| --- |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**Template**, **output**, **package**, and **project** are the required data for each property file.

The following parameters are reset when finishing generating a report for each property file. Other parameter values affect subsequent property files, if there are no overridden values.

- -project
- -output
- -template
- -templatePath
- -tag
- -pVersion
- -recursive
- -category
- -includeAuxiliary

#### TIP: Note

Tip

When large-size projects need to be loaded and closed for many property files, it takes longer time to generate reports. In this case, you can use**-leaveprojectopen** as an option to reduce memory for loading each project when starting to generate a new report.

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

*-leaveprojectopen* needs to be specified before*-properties.*

**Related pages**

- Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server
- Properties filename
- Uploading generated reports to servers
- Syntax rules

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23a1b064-e8ce-40bf-880b-509d05b3c64f" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b2517a1-728c-469a-a681-8cb8134a4b39"><ac:parameter ac:name="">GenerateCommand</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Using the Generate command</strong></h4><p>You can enter the Generate command in the command line to generate a report.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate a report from the package scope</p><hr /><ul><li><pre>generate - project projectFileName -output outputFileName -template templateName -package packageNameList</pre></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To generate a report from the element scope</p><hr /><ul><li><pre>generate -project projectFileName -output outputFileName -template templateName -element elementNameList</pre></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To generate a report from the option specified in the properties file</p><hr /><ul><li><pre>generate -properties propertiesFileName</pre></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To generate an encrypted password for a server project</p><hr /><ul><li><pre>generate -generatepassword password</pre></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11e3de0c-ae60-433e-b8e3-2382ebf610bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Description</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Description </strong></h4><p>The Generate command creates a report document with a received set of information from arguments and parameters. The information will then be generated as a report document to the specified output file. By default, an argument is the specified data of the invoked parameters. If the -properties option is specified, the argument is the name of a properties file. A properties file contains other parameters, along with the specified data of each parameter.</p><p>Synopsis</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 93.582%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 16.6569%;" /><col class="" style="width: 83.3431%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Command</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-project projectFileName</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies the project path as a file name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8fe3091-56b9-4a29-87c1-be341c2458fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify a project name and its branch after <strong>-project</strong>, for example:</p><ul><li>Specifying the <em>MyProject</em> name alone means generating <em>MyProject</em> without specifying its branch.</li><li>Specifying <em>MyProject##release</em> means generating <em>MyProject</em> from the <em>[release]</em> branch.</li><li>Specifying <em>MyProject##release##sp1</em> means generating <em>MyProject</em> from the <em>[release]</em> branch and <em>[sp1]</em> sub-branch.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-output outputFileName</pre></td><td>Specifies the output file path as a file name.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-template templateName</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies a template name used to generate a report document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca4b9530-50a8-427c-97d1-919cd7aea3ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The template must exist in the modeling tool prior to using this command, otherwise, an error will occur.</li><li>If the template name is not specified, the template specified in the Report Data (specified in the <strong>-report </strong>parameter) will be used instead.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-templatePath templatePath</pre></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies a template path used to generate a report document. Both absolute and relative paths are allowed for this command. The specified template file must have proper structure as it has been added through the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog. The <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">folder (directory) of the template must be defined.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39131da4-07ce-4ca7-a3c0-c20c0455379a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If both <strong>-template</strong> and <strong>-templatePath</strong> are specified in the same command, the error will occur.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-package packageNameList</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies the name of one or more packages in a project to be included in the report. A semicolon must separate multiple package entries (;).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f117dbed-06e7-47dd-9a4e-d99006ab5a84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning: Duplicate qualified name</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not enter a duplicate name of a root Model package or its immediate child package to the -package option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">element<span> </span></span>elementQualifiedNameList</pre></td><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>Specifies the qualified name of one or more elements from a project to be included in a report. A semicolon must separate element entries (;).</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-elementID</pre></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies the element ID of one or more elements in a project to be included in the report. A semicolon must separate multiple element entries (;).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5023b2b7-5c49-4ba0-b10b-abeab850766d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Element ID can be retrieved in:<ul><li>Modeling tool element Specifications dialog under All Properties mode.</li><li>Velocity Template Language by calling <strong>$element.elementID</strong> for <strong>$element</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The element ID will be changed when the local project is added to a server. Please make sure the element ID is up-to-date.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>[options]</pre></td><td><p>Command-line options. </p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre><span>-properties propertiesFileName</span></pre></td><td colspan="1">Specifies the name of a properties file to utilize. Use only with -properties.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-generatepassword password</pre></td><td>Generates an encrypted password to be used with a properties file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7554e345-c388-435f-8ab7-f2dbdab52cfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Options</strong></h4><p>The command line feature supports a set of options useful in adding additional configuration possibilities.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 27.1396%;" /><col class="" style="width: 72.8749%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Command</th><th colspan="1">Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-report reportDataName|reportDataElementID</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies the name of a <ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithReportdata"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Data]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> file or Report Data element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c51a68e-6150-4ec6-8cc3-3b74eab3eb12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p>The Report Data file must exist in the modeling tool prior to using this command, otherwise an error will occur.</p></li><li><p>The value for this command can be either a Report Data name or Report Data element ID.</p></li><li><p>If the <strong>-report</strong> parameter is not specified, the previous Report Data will be set and used instead.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-autoImage 0|1|2|3</pre></td><td><p>Specifies how an image will be shown in a report document. The default value for this argument is <strong>1</strong>.</p><ul><li>0 – No resize.</li><li>1 – Fit image to paper (large only).</li><li>2 – Fit and rotate image (clockwise) to paper (large only).</li><li>3 – Fit and rotate image (counter-clockwise) to paper (large only)</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-imageFormat jpg|png|svg|emf|wmf</pre></td><td><p>Specifies an image format in a report document. The default value for this argument is <strong>jpg</strong>.</p><ul><li><em>jpg</em> – Joint Photographic Expert Group</li><li><em>png</em> – Portable Network Graphics</li><li><em>svg</em> – Scaling Vector Image</li><li><em>emf</em> – Microsoft Windows Enhanced Metafile</li><li><em>wmf</em> – Windows Metafile</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-recursive true|false</pre></td><td><p>Specifies how to select a package in a project. The default value for this argument is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> – Select the specified package and its recursive package.</li><li><strong>false</strong> – Select only the specified package.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-includeAuxiliary true|false</pre></td><td>Selects packages, including auxiliary packages. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-outputOnBlankField stringValue</pre></td><td>Shows a string value in a blank field in a report document. The default value for this argument is “”.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-category categoryName</pre></td><td>Specifies a template category.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-fields[name=value]</pre></td><td>Specifies a variable.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-spassword encryptedPassword</pre></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies an encrypted password to log on to the server. This option is available only in a properties file.</p><p>You can get encrypted password by entering this command below in command line prompt.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d67a932d-8f64-4d04-bcb5-1d2d547b650e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -generatepassword "enter_your_password_here"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-upload serverAddress</pre></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specifies a serverAddress consisting of a scheme, userInfo, host, port, and path to connect and upload a generated report to a server (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Uploading generated reports to servers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Upload generated report to server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90772485-a785-41b4-9f51-8e75e68e2281"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case the server has not been trusted, a message will be displayed in the command line, asking you to choose to accept or reject the server certificate. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-trustAll true|false</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p>Trusts any certificate of the specific publishing server in -upload command. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><p>If the value is <strong>true</strong>, the certificate will be trusted without <span>asking </span>any questions.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-overwrite true|false</pre></td><td><p>Overwrites any previously generated files. The default value for this argument is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> – Overwrites all files automatically (removes any existing file before generating a report.)</li><li><strong>false</strong> – If the file exists, the following prompt message will open:</li></ul><table data-mce-resize="false"><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>Output file already exists.</pre><pre>Would you like to replace the existing file? [y][n]:</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p>If you type ‘y’ or ‘yes’, the Report Wizard will generate a new report that replaces the previous one. If you type ‘n’ or ‘no’, the Report Wizard will not generate a report and terminate the process.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-pversion projectVersion</pre></td><td>Specifies a server project version. </td></tr><tr class=""><td><pre>-ssl true|false </pre></td><td><p>Specifies if a Secured Connection (SSL) is used. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> - Use Secured Connection (SSL)</li><li><strong>false</strong> - Do not use Secured Connection (SSL)</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-tag tagValue</pre></td><td colspan="1"><span>An additional argument to generate reports from a server project by specifying a server project tag name. </span><span>The tagValue is non-case sensitive. More than one project version may be returned from specifying one tag value. </span><span>Hence, one report generation process may generate multiple reports. You cannot use this command with –pversion.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-incremental true|false</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p>Specifies the incremental method. The default value is <strong>true</strong>.</p><ul class="alternate"><li><p><strong>true</strong> - The output report shall be generated by appending a new generated output file name with the incremental number.</p></li><li><p><strong>false</strong> - The latest project version in the returned project collection. Use this command with -tag only.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-leaveprojectopen</pre></td><td colspan="1">Leaves the project open per property file after generating the report, in a series of report generation. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-loadmodule true|false</pre></td><td colspan="1"><p>Loads all unloaded modules in a project. The default value is <strong>false</strong>.</p><ul><li><strong>true</strong> - All modules including unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project.</li><li><strong>false</strong> - All modules except unloaded modules are loaded while opening a project.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><pre>-projectpassword projectPassword</pre></td><td colspan="1"><span>Specifies a password for the protected Teamwork Cloud project. The </span><span>password is required to </span><span>open a protected Teamwork Cloud project for generating a report.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb5d27c3-e73f-4227-b0d3-db5f070fea18"><ac:parameter ac:name="">UsingCommand</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong>Using command line</strong></h4><p>You can use the command line on Windows, Linux, or Unix.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use the command line on Windows</p><hr /><ol><li>Run a command line console. In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.</li><li>Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To use the command line on Linux or Unix</p><hr /><ol><li>Run a terminal (command line console). In the Console window, go to the local installation of the modeling tool's application.</li><li>Go to the plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard folder, and type the command line there.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>See the following examples of command line on Windows and Linux or Unix respectively.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d05e7b-d85d-4d58-af06-e3a97955dc57"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -properties "C:\\output\\prop.properties"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9372603-379a-4b2c-80eb-6f11de66f4d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Windows</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "C:\\MagicDraw\\samples\\diagrams\\class diagram.mdzip" -output "C:\\output\\output.rtf" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -report "Built-in" -autoImage 1 -imageFormat png -recursive false -outputOnBlankField "#NA" -fields "[Author=NMA][Company=No Magic]"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2715ac73-abb4-455f-af9b-c8bd01b544be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On Linux or Unix</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[./generate.sh -project "/home/project/project.mdzip" -output "/home/output/output.rtf" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -report "Built-in" -autoImage 1 -imageFormat png -recursive false -outputOnBlankField "#NA" -fields "[Author=NMA][Company=No Magic]"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7388279-fb41-4326-a5d2-5b618215afa1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">MultipleReports</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Generating multiple reports </h4><p>You can open multiple projects and generate multiple reports before the modeling tool shuts down. The <strong>generate</strong> command with the -properties option accepts multiple properties files as shown below.</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties &lt;file1.properties&gt; &lt;file2.properties&gt; &lt;file3.properties&gt;...</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td>generate -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f51f4e8-3acb-491d-b2d8-9f3171611a39"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Template</strong>, <strong>output</strong>, <strong>package</strong>, and <strong>project</strong> are the required data for each property file.</p><p>The following parameters are reset when finishing generating a report for each property file. Other parameter values affect subsequent property files, if there are no overridden values.</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">-project</p></li><li>-output</li><li>-template</li><li>-templatePath</li><li>-tag</li><li>-pVersion</li><li>-recursive</li><li>-category</li><li>-includeAuxiliary</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="417ea8b0-2a85-4a3b-87a0-f818565da74c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When large-size projects need to be loaded and closed for many property files, it takes longer time to generate reports. In this case, you can use<strong> -leaveprojectopen</strong> as an option to reduce memory for loading each project when starting to generate a new report.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a6aa847-76e1-46dd-bcc5-cd6809a96045"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -leaveprojectopen true -properties “property1.properties” “property2.properties”]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da55347-e339-4281-b8fb-fb0ac99bba30"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><em>-leaveprojectopen</em> needs to be specified before<em> -properties.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server">Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Properties+filename">Properties filename</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Uploading+generated+reports+to+servers">Uploading generated reports to servers</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR1/Syntax+rules">Syntax rules</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169674787 space=MD2024xR1 version=1 -->
## PAGE 01248: Using the grid

- page_id: `169674787`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169674787/Using+the+grid
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-09-23T05:59:26.669+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

903695269

903695272

903695271

The grid helps to arrange diagram symbols on the diagram pane. By default, the grid is not visible.

To show the grid on the diagram, do one of the following

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Show Grid .
- From the diagram shortcut menu, select Show Grid .
- In the Diagram Properties dialog, set the Show Grid property to true .

To snap the paths to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Paths to Grid .

To snap the shapes to gridlines

- From the main menu, select View > Grid > Snap Shapes to Grid .

To change the grid size

1. From the main menu, select View > Grid > Grid Size . The Grid Size dialog opens.
2. Enter a grid size between 2 and 30 (default is 10).
3. Click OK .

To change the grid style

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the Diagram group, from the Grid Style option drop-down list, select one of the following styles:
  - Dense
  - Sparse (default)

903695268

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b3ab661-6465-4de6-acbc-ed12fe2fd9d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903695269</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24d3f50d-e122-4e4a-b98e-98904ce62061"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903695272</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5abd9b6-efc4-4539-abef-c5dd58e78438"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903695271</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The grid helps to arrange diagram symbols on the diagram pane. By default, the grid is not visible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show the grid on the diagram, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Grid</strong>.</li><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Grid</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Grid</strong> property to <em>true</em>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To snap the paths to gridlines</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Snap Paths to Grid</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To snap the shapes to gridlines</p><hr /><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong>Snap Shapes to Grid</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the grid size</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Grid</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Grid Size</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Grid Size</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> dialog opens. </span></li><li>Enter a grid size between 2 and 30 (default is 10).</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change the grid style</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagram </strong>group, from the <strong>Grid Style</strong> option drop-down list, select one of the following styles:<ul><li>Dense </li><li>Sparse (default)</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8085ad5-38e6-4b66-a513-d5c6d6b37b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">903695268</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673258 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01249: Using the Model Browser

- page_id: `169673258`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673258/Using+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:47.855+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425704795

1425704797

1425704796

INLINE

Model Browser is displayed when at least one project is open. By default, the Model Browser is placed on the left side of the main window. It is possible to move the Model Browser to any place on the window. Also, all tabs can be viewed separately and you can set up the Model Browser according to your needs by hiding the desired tabs. 
 
To change the size of either part of the Model Browser

- Drag the bar that separates the two parts.

To zoom the Model Browser

1. Select a tab in the Model Browser.
2. Use one of the following shortcuts:

- 
  - For Mac

1. 
  1. 
    1. Cmd+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Cmd+Numpad +/-

- 
  - For Windows

1. 
  1. 
    1. Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Ctrl+Numpad +/-

To change the Model Browser position

- Using the **Options** menu

1. 
  1. From the **Options** menu, select **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog opens.
  2. Click on the **Browser** option group.
  3. In the Browser environment options pane, change the **Browser Position** property to **Right** or **Left**.

- Using Toggle 
In the upper-right corner of the selected tab in the Model Browser, click the Toggle auto-hide [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or Toggle floating [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. This action changes the layout of the interface.

To close or reopen the desired tab of the Model Browser

- Using the **Window** menu, choose the Model Browser tab you want to close or open.

To reset all Model Browser tabs to the default position

- From the **Window** menu, choose the **Reset Windows Configuration** command.

To sort items in the Model Browser alphabetically

1. From the Options menu, select Environment. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Click the Browser option group.
3. In the Browser options pane, set the Sort Alphabetically value to true (the default value is true).

1425704794

**Related pages**

- Model Browser
