<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169677554 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01250: Using Used Projects Auto Update Wizard

- page_id: `169677554`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169677554/Using+Used+Projects+Auto+Update+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-11-18T06:17:27.659+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Used Projects Auto Update Plugin
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section describes how to use the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard to configure and generate properties file.

#### NOTE: Use dedicated user

Use dedicated user

After you enable the plugin, it is highly recommended that you log in to the Teamwork Cloud environment in the modeling tool with a dedicated user. This is the only way to ensure that all dedicated user permissions are checked and the properties file will be set correctly.

After the plugin is enabled in the **Project Usages**dialog you will see a new button: **Used Projects Auto Update Wizard** [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To open the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard

1. Make sure the plugin is enabled.
2. Login with the dedicated user, and only then open the project in order to continue using the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard.
3. To open a wizard, go to the Project Usages dialog.
4. Click on the button [ATTACHMENT filename='Used_project_update_wizard.png'] Used Projects Auto Update Wizard . The wizard dialog opens.

To configure a properties file with the Used Projects Auto Update Wizard

1. In the opened Used Projects Auto Update Wizard dialog, you can see the username you are logged in with. This user will be set up for the properties file to automatically update used projects.
2. In the Properties file field, you need to select a file and location to overwrite or save the new properties file that will be generated for the plugin.
3. After you have selected a properties file, click Next .
4. Select directly and indirectly used projects. Values listed in the Status column show whether or not a used project can be updated.
5. After you select the used projects, click Generate to create the properties file that will be used later by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating a used project with a command line' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Used Projects Auto Update Wizard dialogs.

To see where the selected used project is used, click the **Project Usage Information** button. This will help to find update paths. An *update path* is a path of projects that must be updated in order to update a certain used project.

##### Possible errors and warnings with explanations

#### NOTE: Warning: Causes version conflict with at least one used project.

Warning: Causes version conflict with at least one used project.

This warning indicates that in this project, there is at least one other unselected used project that is using the same project. In order to achieve consistency on the model, we recommend updating all used projects that use this particular project.

#### WARNING: ERROR: Cannot be updated due to insufficient permissions.

ERROR: Cannot be updated due to insufficient permissions.

This error indicates that the user who is logged in does not have enough permissions to update this particular project. Please add required permissions for the user in order to update this project.

#### WARNING: ERROR: Cannot be updated due to read-only using project

ERROR: Cannot be updated due to read-only using project

This error indicates that the used project is being used in a read-only project (e.g. public branch); thus, we can not commit a new version of a read-only project in order to update the selected project. Please use this project directly or in another project with a branch for which the user has read-writepermissions.

#### WARNING: ERROR: Cannot be updated due to cyclic usage.

ERROR: Cannot be updated due to cyclic usage.

This error indicates that the used project has cyclic dependencies between projects. Please remove any used projects from this used project that would cause a cycle between projects.

#### WARNING: An example

ERROR: Cannot be updated due to missing update path.

This error can appear on indirectly used projects due to a missing update path. An *update path* is a path of projects that must be updated in order to update a certain used project. The following example explains this term:

#### TIP: An example

An example

A Main Project uses project A. Project A uses Project B. In order to update Project B in the Main Project, we are required to update Project B in Project A first and then update Project A in the Main Project. Thus, the update path for Project B is: Main Project > Project A.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169673717 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01251: Validating project integrity

- page_id: `169673717`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169673717/Validating+project+integrity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T11:59:50.774+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

762190336

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

762190338

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

762190337

Some aspects are validated automatically, such as:

- Mount location inconsistencies in used projects
- Required version inconsistencies in used projects

##### Mount location inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when packages of the used project have different mount locations defined in separate projects.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* has mounted a package of the used project *ComponentLibrary*, while the used project *MainBlocks* has also mounted the same package *ComponentsLibrary*. As shown in the following figure, the mount location conflict occurs because the project*RadioReceiverProject*is using both the *Tools*and *MainBlocks*used projects at the same time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='MD2024xR1']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the**Mounted On**value in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting mount locations warning displayed in Used Project dialog.

##### Required version inconsistencies in used projects

This occurs when a project directly and indirectly uses several different versions of the same used project at the same time.

#### INFO: Example

Example

The used project *Tools* is using the 4th version of the used project *ComponentLibrary* and the used project *MainBlocks* is using the 2nd version of the used project *ComponentLibrary*. In the project *RadioReceiverProject*, a used project *ComponentLibrary* version conflict occurs as two different versions of this project are required at the same time (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can solve the conflict in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR1']**U****sed Project**dialogby unifying the required versions in both projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of conflicting versions warning displayed in Used Project dialog

##### Cyclic dependencies among used projects

The figure below illustrates an example of cyclic dependency:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of cyclic dependency.

The Project Integrity Correctness suite includes the rules for **detecting** Cyclic dependencies among used projects.While [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1']the model, use the **Project Integrity Correctness** validation suite to find cyclic dependency issues in your project composition.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project Integrity Correctness suite

Violations of your project integrity will be displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of violations detected while checking project integrity correctness

You can resolve the cyclic dependency issues in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR1']**Used Project** dialog or you can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024xR1'].

762197964

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='MD2024xR1']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13da8e92-dc44-4747-b286-9081f1167f78"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762190336</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd1d4268-d452-40d4-bdf7-d88ab3c25f5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762190338</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d3dd2f-dc7d-478e-a931-d29feb0d1376"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762190337</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Some aspects are validated automatically, such as:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Mount location inconsistencies in used projects</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Required version inconsistencies in used projects</span></li></ul><h3>Mount location inconsistencies in used projects</h3><p>This occurs when packages of the used project have different mount locations defined in separate projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8403e3a7-7224-4430-b212-b201ee6fa62e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The used project <em>Tools</em> has mounted a package of the used project <em>ComponentLibrary</em>, while the used project <em>MainBlocks</em> has also mounted the same package <em>ComponentsLibrary</em>. <span>As shown in the following figure, the mount location conflict occurs because the project </span><em>RadioReceiverProject</em><span> is using both the <em>Tools</em></span><span> and <em>MainBlocks </em></span><span>used projects at the same time.</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="mount_location_inconsistency_conflict.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>You can solve the conflict in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:link-body><span><strong>U</strong></span><span><strong>sed Project</strong> </span><span>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span> by unifying the </span><strong>Mounted On </strong><span>value in both projects</span><span>. </span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_conflicting_mount_locations.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of conflicting mount locations warning displayed in Used Project dialog.</h6><h3>Required version inconsistencies in used projects</h3><p>This occurs when a project directly and indirectly uses several different versions of the same used project at the same time.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70f61969-e55e-43ab-bf82-80ba3e23c42e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The used project <em>Tools</em> is using the 4th version of the used project <em>ComponentLibrary</em> and the used project <em>MainBlocks</em> is using the 2nd version of the used project <em>ComponentLibrary</em>. In the project <em>RadioReceiverProject</em>, a used project <em>ComponentLibrary</em> version conflict occurs as <span>two different versions of this project are required at the same time</span> (see the following figure). </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="required_version_inconsistency_confilct.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>You can solve the conflict in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><span><strong>U</strong></span><span><strong>sed Project</strong> </span><span>dialog</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span> by unifying the required versions in both projects</span><span>. </span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_conflicting_versions_warning.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Example of conflicting versions warning displayed in Used Project dialog</span></h6><h3>Cyclic dependencies among used projects</h3><p>The figure below illustrates an example of cyclic dependency:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cyclic_dependency.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Example of cyclic dependency.</span></h6><p>The Project Integrity Correctness suite includes the rules for <strong>detecting</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Cyclic dependencies among used projects. </span>While <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validating ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the model, use the <strong>Project Integrity Correctness</strong> validation suite to find cyclic dependency issues in your project composition.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_integrity_correctness_suite.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Project Integrity Correctness suite </h6><p>Violations of your project integrity will be displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning_cyclic_dependencies.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validating project integrity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of violations detected while checking project integrity correctness </h6><p><br /></p><p>You can resolve the cyclic dependency issues in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> or you can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR1" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /></ac:link>.</p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c16c0f5f-62ad-458c-b834-99d480064270"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762197964</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation Results Panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR1" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Used Projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=169676907 space=MD2024xR1 version=2 -->
## PAGE 01252: Validation

- page_id: `169676907`
- space_key: `MD2024xR1`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR1/pages/169676907/Validation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-05-08T12:00:22.804+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: ['validation', 'active-validation', 'validation-rules', 'validation-suites']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Solvers of invalid elements

1351877105

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Solvers of invalid elements

1351877107

INLINE

927119908

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Solvers of invalid elements

INLINE

##### Purpose

Validation checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, marks invalid elements in the model, and suggests solutions.

##### Validation suites

Models are validated against a set of validation rules. Each validation rule captures an imperative condition that must be checked against the model. Validation rules are grouped into meaningful groups called validation suites. There are predefined validation suites that you can customize. You can also create your own rules and group them into suites. Some validation rules are active; that is, they run all the time. Validation rules and suites can be exported into a separate project in order to reuse them. They are stored in the Teamwork Cloud server for exchange.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined validation suites' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active validation suites' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- Learn more about SysML active validation suites >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirements validation suites' space='CRMP2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new validation rules' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global validation rules' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

##### Validating your model

The validation can run on the entire model or on a selected part of it. All the elements in that scope are checked against relevant validation rules, and elements that violate these rules are reported in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel. They are also marked in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'], and their symbols are highlighted. Diagrams, tables, matrices, and relation maps displaying these highlighted symbols have the validation results marker bar.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation results marker bar' space='MD2024xR1']Learn more about validation results marker bar >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']Learn more about Validation Results panel >>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-commit validation' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

##### Invalid elements representation

The invalid elements are marked as follows:

- [ATTACHMENT filename='error_icon.png'] - error or fatal error.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='warning_icon.png'] - warning.
- [ATTACHMENT filename='info_icon.png'] - debug or info.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='MD2024xR1']>]]>

You can see invalid elements in the following areas:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR1'] an invalid element is marked with a small symbol that depends on the failure severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_element_in_model_brower.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] an invalid symbol is highlighted. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_on_diagram_pane.png']
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation results marker bar' space='MD2024xR1'] every marker of an invalid symbol is colored according to the violation severity. [ATTACHMENT filename='invalid_symbol_in_validation_results_marker_bar.png']
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR1'] the failure indicator displays the severity icon, number of errors, and the first letter of the error severity (F - fatal error; E - error; W - warning; D - debug; I - info). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Solvers of invalid elements

Solvers of invalid elements

You can analyze invalid elements and solve problems by selecting appropriate commands from the invalid element or symbol shortcut menu or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='MD2024xR1']**Validation Results** panel.

1351877103
